Description: Facilities that provide open access to customers.  Includes teller windows, safe deposit boxes, ATMs, and other such facilities.

Name: Savings Institution
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in accepting time deposits, making mortgage and real estate loans, and investing in high-grade securities.

Name: Savings Institution Office Building
Description: Facilities holding the administrative office of the institution.  Includes operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Savings Institution Customer Service Facility
Description: Facilities that provide open access to customers.  Includes teller windows, safe deposit boxes, and other such facilities.

Name: Credit Union
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in accepting members' share deposits in cooperatives that are organized to offer consumer loans to their members.

Name: Credit Union Office Building
Description: Facilities holding the administrative office of the institution. Includes operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Credit Union Customer Service Facility
Description: Facilities that provide open access to customers.  Includes teller windows, safe deposit boxes, and other such services.

Name: Other Depository Credit Institution
Description: Other establishments primarily engaged in accepting deposits and lending funds not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Other Depository Credit Institution Office Building
Description: Facilities holding the administrative office of the institution. Includes operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Other Depository Credit Institution Customer Service Facility
Description: Facilities that provide open access to customers.  Includes teller windows, safe deposit boxes, and other such services.

Name: Non-Depository Credit Institution
Description: Establishments, both public (government-sponsored enterprises) and private, primarily engaged in extending credit or lending funds raised by credit market borrowing, such as issuing commercial paper or other debt instruments or by borrowing from other financial intermediaries.

Name: Credit Card Company
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing credit by issuing credit cards. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Consumer Lending
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in making unsecured cash loans to consumers. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Business Lending
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in lending funds to businesses (with or without real estate as collateral). Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Real Estate Credit
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in lending funds with real estate as collateral. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: International Trade Financing
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing working capital funds to U.S. exporters lending funds to foreign buyers of U.S. goods and/or lending funds to domestic buyers of imported goods. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Government Sponsored Enterprise
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in buying, pooling, and repackaging loans for sale to others on the secondary market. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Other Non-Depository Credit Institution
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing non-depository credit other than those itemized above. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Banking or Credit Supporting Facility
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in facilitating credit intermediation by performing activities, such as arranging loans by bringing borrowers and lenders together and clearing checks and credit card transactions.

Name: Loan Brokerage
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in arranging loans by bringing borrowers and lenders together on a commission or fee basis. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Financial Transaction Processing or Clearinghouse Facility
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing financial transaction processing (except central bank) reserve and liquidity services (except central bank) and/or check or other financial instrument clearinghouse services (except central bank). Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Banking and Credit Emergency Redundancy Service Facility
Description: Facilities that provide backup capability for employees and data, information, and telecommunications networks to allow banking and credit institutions to continue functioning in the event of a disruption to their primary facilities.

Name: Banking and Credit Company Emergency Redundancy Service Facility
Description: Facilities that are owned and operated by the company utilizing them.

Name: Banking and Credit Contract Emergency Redundancy Service Facility
Description: Facilities that are owned and operated by a service provider.  Services may be offered to a number of companies.

Name: Physical Repository
Description: Secured facilities where large quantities of currency and/or precious metals are stored.  Includes both government and private repositories.

Name: Banking and Credit Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Organizations that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight and support to banking and credit organizations.

Name: Federal Banking or Credit Agency
Description: Federal agencies that deal with the banking and credit system including Department of Treasury, Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, and others. Note: Federal Reserve System is itemized separately in 2.1.1.

Name: State, Local, Regional Banking or Credit Agency
Description: State, local, and regional entities dealing with banking and credit in their jurisdiction including banking departments, consumer protection departments, and others.

Name: Banking and Credit Industry Organization
Description: Industry organizations that provide industry-wide support.

Name: International Banking and Credit Organization
Description: International organizations dealing with banking and credit issues.

Name: Securities, Commodities, or Financial Investment
Description: Establishments that are primarily engaged in underwriting securities issues and/or making markets for securities and commodities acting as agents (e.g., brokers) between buyers and sellers of securities and commodities, providing securities and commodities exchange services, and providing other services, such as managing portfolios of assets, providing investment advice, and trust, fiduciary, and custody services.

Name: Securities and Commodities Broker
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in putting capital at risk in the process of underwriting securities issues or in making markets for securities and commodities and those acting as agents and/or brokers between buyers and sellers of securities and commodities, usually charging a commission. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Securities and Commodities Exchange
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in furnishing physical or electronic marketplaces for the purpose of facilitating the buying and selling of stocks, stock options, bonds, or commodities contracts. Includes administrative offices, exchange floors, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Other Financial Investment Facility
Description: Facilities that include portfolio managers, investment fund managers, mutual fund managers, pension fund managers, investment advice, quotation services, trust and estate managers, and others.

Name: Securities, Commodities, or Financial Investment Supporting Facility
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in facilitating securities, commodities, and other financial investments by performing supporting activities.

Name: Securities, Commodities, or Financial Investment Processing and Clearinghouse Activity
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing financial transaction processing, clearinghouse services, and quotations. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Securities and Commodities Emergency Redundancy Service
Description: Facilities that provide backup capability for employees and data, information, telecommunications networks to allow securities, commodities, and financial investment institutions to continue functioning in the event of a disruption to their primary facilities.

Name: Securities and Commodities Company Emergency Facilities Redundancy Service Facility
Description: Facilities that are owned and operated by the company utilizing them.

Name: Securities and Commodities Contract Emergency Redundancy Service Facility
Description: Facilities that are owned and operated by a service provider. Services may be offered to a number of companies.

Name: Securities, Commodities, Financial Investment Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Organizations that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight and support to securities, commodities, and financial investment organizations.

Name: Federal Securities, Commodities, or Financial Investment Agency
Description: Federal agencies that deal with the securities, commodities, and financial investment industry.

Name: State, Local, Regional Securities, Commodities, or Financial Investment Agency
Description: State, local, and regional entities dealing with the securities, commodities, and financial investment industry in their jurisdiction.

Name: Securities, Commodities, or Financial Investment Industry Organization
Description: Industry organizations that provide industry-wide support.

Name: International Securities, Commodities, or Financial Investment Organization
Description: International organizations dealing with securities, commodities, and financial investment issues.

Name: Insurance Company
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in underwriting (assuming the risk, assigning premiums, and so forth) annuities and insurance policies, or facilitating such underwriting by selling insurance policies, and by providing other insurance and employee-benefit related services.

Name: Direct Insurance Company
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in initially underwriting and assuming the risk of annuities and insurance policies. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Reinsurance Company
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in assuming all or part of the risk associated with existing insurance policies originally underwritten by other insurance carriers. Includes administrative offices, operations centers, data and information systems, and telecommunication systems.

Name: Insurance Producer
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in acting as agents (e.g., brokers) in selling annuities and insurance policies.

Name: Insurance Support Organization
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in facilitating insurance activities by performing supporting activities. Includes claims adjustment, claims investigation, claims processing, actuarial services, loss prevention services, rate making services, and medical cost evaluation.

Name: Insurance Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Organizations that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight and support to insurance organizations.

Name: Federal Insurance Agency
Description: Federal agencies that deal with the insurance industry.

Name: State, Local, Regional Insurance Agency
Description: State, local, and regional agencies dealing with insurance activities in their jurisdiction.

Name: Insurance Industry Organization
Description: Industry organizations that provide industry-wide support.

Name: International Insurance Organization
Description: International organizations dealing with insurance issues.

Name: Chemical and Hazardous Materials Industry
Description: The Chemical Sector consists of facilities that transform natural, raw materials obtained from the earth, sea and air into products that are used every day, to include the transportation of these chemicals to intermediate or end users.

Name: Chemical Manufacturing or Processing Plant
Description: Assets that manufacture or process chemicals by transforming natural raw materials from the earth, sea and air into products that are used daily.

Name: Basic Chemical Production
Description: Assets involved in chemical production of large volumes resulting in chemical composition specifications that are homogeneous in nature. There is no product differentiation.

Name: Inorganic Chemical Production
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals generally derived from metal and non-metallics (including salt). Inorganic chemicals do not generally contain carbon as a principal element.

Name: Alkali and Chlorine
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals generally derived from alkali, a basic, ionic salt of an alkali metal or alkaline earth metal element, or chlorine. (Alkali is a basic, ionic salt of an alkali metal or alkaline earth metal element, also defined as a base that dissolves in water. A solution of a soluble base that has a pH greater than 7. Chlorine is an element, halogen, commonly found as an ion in salt.)

Name: Industrial Gas
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals generally derived from industrial gases, which are a group of gases that are commercially manufactured and sold for uses in other applications.  These gases are mainly used in industrial processes. They may be both organic and inorganic, are produced by extraction from the air by a process of separation or are produced by chemical synthesis, and will take various forms such as compressed, liquid, or solid.

Name: Inorganic Pigment
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals generally derived from an inorganic pigment (a material that changes the color of reflected or transmitted light as the result of wavelength-selective absorption).

Name: Acid
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals generally derived from acids (a substance that tastes sour, reacts with metals and carbonates, turns blue litmus paper red, and has a pH less than 7.0 in its standard state).

Name: Other Inorganic Material
Description: Inorganic chemical production facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Petrochemical and Derivative
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals generally derived from organic chemicals and other chemical products derived from bulk petrochemicals, organic intermediates and other sources of carbon molecules. In many cases, these chemicals are compounded with inorganic chemicals and other materials. These products in turn are used in downstream derivatives.

Name: Organic Chemical
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals generally derived from an organic compound whose molecules contain carbon.

Name: Synthetic Material
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals generally derived from material not made from natural (animal, plant) materials.

Name: Other Organic Material
Description: Petrochemical and derivative facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Other Industrial Chemical
Description: Basic chemical production facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Specialty Chemical Production
Description: Assets engaged in manufacturing differentiated products, or low-volume, high-value compounds sold on the basis of what they do, not what they are. These are generally blended with other compounds according to proprietary formulations.

Name: Coating
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals used as materials applied to surfaces to protect and/or decorate.

Name: Explosive
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals that contain a great amount of stored energy that can produce an explosion and/or a sudden expansion of the material after initiation, usually accompanied by the production or light, heat, sound and pressure.

Name: Other Specialty
Description: Other specialty chemical production facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Agricultural Chemical Production
Description: Assets that manufacture or process chemicals used mainly in the farming industry as fertilizers or for crop protection.

Name: Fertilizer
Description: Assets involved in the production of various combinations of nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium that are added to soil to replace or supplement essential nutrients to promote plant growth.

Name: Crop Protection
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemical products that help control weeds, harmful insect pests, and plant diseases that afflict crops, as well as products used to control germs and pests.

Name: Pesticide
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemical products intended to prevent, destroy, repel, or mitigate any pest.

Name: Herbicide
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemical products used to kill unwanted plants.

Name: Other Crop Protection Chemical
Description: Crop protection facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Pharmaceutical Production
Description: Assets involved in the production of prescription and over-the-counter drugs; in-vitro and other diagnostic substances to diagnose or monitor the state of human health or veterinary health; bacterial and virus vaccines; toxoids; serums; plasma and other biological products for human and veterinary health; and vitamins and other pharmaceutical preparations for both human and veterinary use. This segment also includes biotechnology, with applications in crop seeds, traits and value-added grains and enzymes among others.

Name: Consumer Product
Description: Assets involved in the production of highly differentiated product lines that are packaged along branding lines. These are widely known as household products.

Name: Potential Chemical Weapon Agent and Precursor
Description: Assets involved in the production of chemicals that, due to their dual use characteristics, may represent a security risk as defined under the Chemical Weapons Convention (CWC).

Name: Hazardous Chemical Transport
Description: Assets used to transport hazardous chemicals.

Name: Pipeline
Description: Pipelines used for the transport of hazardous chemicals.

Name: Pipeline Component
Description: Lengths of pipeline, interconnections, and valves. Includes above ground, underground, river crossings, and other segments.

Name: Pipeline Pumping Station
Description: Stations along the length of a pipeline. Includes pumps, valves, control machinery, and breakout storage.

Name: Pipeline Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate a pipeline(s). Includes supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system control centers.

Name: Marine Transport
Description: Marine assets used for transporting hazardous chemicals.

Name: Tanker
Description: Marine vessels for moving hazardous chemicals. Includes ocean-going tankers for movement of imported and domestic hazardous chemicals and tankers and barges for movement of hazardous chemicals on coastal and inland waterways.

Name: Port Handling Hazardous Chemical
Description: Port facilities for mooring, docking, loading, and unloading marine vessels with hazardous chemicals. Includes shuttle tankers to off-load larger vessels.

Name: Rail Transport
Description: Railroad assets used for transporting hazardous chemicals.

Name: Railroad Tank Car
Description: Railroad tank cars designed for carrying hazardous chemicals. Note: Railroad tank cars are sometimes used as mobile storage: that is, the product is not off-loaded into a stationary tank but is used directly from the tank car.

Name: Railroad Loading Rack
Description: The shelter and associated equipment for the withdrawal of liquid petroleum or a chemical product from a storage tank and loading it into a railroad tank car or tank truck.

Name: Road Transport
Description: Road assets used for transporting hazardous chemicals.

Name: Tanker Truck
Description: Trucks (generally semi-trailers) designed to carry hazardous chemicals.

Name: Tanker Truck Loading Rack
Description: Facilities at some storage facilities and chemical plants that are used to load tanker trucks that deliver chemicals.

Name: Air Transport
Description: Aviation facilities and assets (e.g., aircraft, airports) involved in the transport of hazardous chemicals by air.

Name: Hazardous Chemical Storage/Stockpile/Utilization/Distribution
Description: Facilities or assets that do not manufacture hazardous chemicals but have hazardous chemicals on-site, in storage, for use in other manufacturing operations, or for an end use activity. Facilities that manufacture hazardous chemicals are included in the Hazardous Chemical Manufacturing or Processing Plant sub-sector (3.1).

Name: Warehousing and Storage
Description: Facilities or assets primarily engaged in operating warehousing and storage facilities for hazardous chemicals and potentially other warehouse products. May or may not sell the goods they handle. May provide a range of services, often referred to as logistics services, related to the distribution of hazardous chemicals.

Name: Bulk Storage
Description: Facilities or assets used for the storage and/or marketing of hazardous chemicals. Includes storage tanks, pipes and pumps, control machinery, and other equipment. These facilities are not used to store hazardous chemicals for final consumption. Also referred to as tank farms.

Name: Land-Based Bulk Terminal
Description: Facilities or assets that receive and distribute chemicals via pipeline, marine vessel, rail, or truck from either domestic or import suppliers. Includes the valves, piping, control machinery, and other equipment to connect the terminal to the suppliers serving it.

Name: Marine Bulk Terminal
Description: Large terminal located on a waterway. Generally receives and distributes its petroleum via pipeline, barge, or marine tanker from either domestic or foreign suppliers.
CrosssectorReference: 11.4.2.2.5 Deep Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal - Chemical

Name: Retail Distribution
Description: Facilities or assets used to store hazardous chemicals for retail distribution to end use customers.

Name: Storage at Manufacturing Facility
Description: Establishments (other than chemical manufacturers) that use hazardous chemicals in the manufacturing and production of other products. They purchase hazardous chemicals from manufacturers and/or from wholesalers.

Name: Storage at Non-Manufacturing Facility
Description: Establishments that are not manufacturers but that have hazardous chemicals on-site to use in their activities. Note: Facilities in this category are listed in their primary categorization (e.g., wastewater treatment plants). If they have hazardous chemicals on site (e.g., chlorine), this concept is added.

Name: Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Organizations that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight and support to the chemical industry.

Name: Federal Chemicals Oversight Agency
Description: Federal agencies that deal with or regulate the chemical industry.

Name: State, Local, Regional Chemicals Oversight Agency
Description: State, local, and regional agencies regulating chemical companies operating in their jurisdiction including environmental agencies, transportation agencies, and others.

Name: Chemical Industry Organization or Trade Association
Description: Industry organizations that provide industry-wide support.

Name: International Chemical Organization
Description: Industry organizations with worldwide vision and influence for the chemical industry.

Name: Other Chemical and Hazardous Material
Description: Chemical and hazardous materials industry facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Defense Industrial Base
Description: The Department of Defense, government, and private sector industrial complex with capabilities to perform research and development, design, produce, and maintain military weapon systems, subsystems, components and parts to meet military requirements.

Name: Shipbuilding Industry
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in operating a shipyard. Does not include civilian shipyards.

Name: Surface Shipbuilding and Maintenance
Description: Shipyards primarily engaged in building, maintaining, and repairing surface ships.

Name: Subsurface Shipbuilding and Maintenance
Description: Shipyards specializing in building, maintaining, and repairing submarines and other submersibles.

Name: Unmanned Vessel Construction and Maintenance
Description: Shipyards specializing in the construction, maintenance, and repair of unmanned marine vessels.

Name: Aircraft Industry
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing or assembling complete aircraft. Does not include those building civilian aircraft.

Name: Fixed Wing Aircraft Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of fixed wing airplanes.

Name: Helicopter Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of helicopters.

Name: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing of unmanned aircraft.

Name: Missile Industry
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing missile systems.

Name: Air/Air Missile Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of air-to-air missiles.

Name: Air/Surface Missile Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of air-to-surface missiles.

Name: Surface/Air Missile Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of surface-to-air missiles.

Name: Surface/Surface Missile Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of surface-to-surface missiles.

Name: Missile Defense System Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of missile defense systems.

Name: Precision Guided Munitions Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of precision guided munitions (PGM).

Name: Space Industry
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing complete space vehicles.

Name: Launch Vehicle Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing, maintenance, and repair of space launch vehicles.

Name: Satellite Manufacturing
Description: Facilities specializing in the manufacturing of space satellites.

Name: Combat Vehicle Industry
Description: Establishments manufacturing military land vehicles.

Name: Combat Vehicle Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing tanks and other such vehicles intended for use in combat.

Name: Tactical Vehicle Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing tactical vehicles.

Name: Unmanned Vehicle Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing unmanned vehicles.

Name: Ammunition Industry
Description: Establishments manufacturing military ammunition.  Does not include those manufacturing ammunition solely for civilian use.

Name: Artillery Caliber Munitions Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing artillery caliber munitions.

Name: Bomb Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing large military ordinance.

Name: Cannon Caliber Munitions Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing cannon caliber munitions.

Name: Demolition, Grenade, and Mine Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing demolition explosives, grenades, and mines.

Name: Dispenser Munitions Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing dispenser munitions (e.g., munitions that are separated from a parent munition on detonation).

Name: Family of Scatterable Mine Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing scatterable mines.

Name: Fuse Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing military fuses.

Name: Mortar Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing mortars.

Name: Navy Gun Munitions Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing munitions for use in naval guns.

Name: Propellant and Explosives Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing military propellants and explosives.

Name: Propelling Charge Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing military propelling charges.

Name: Pyrotechnics Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing military pyrotechnics.

Name: Rocket and Warhead Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing military rockets and warheads.

Name: Small Caliber Munitions Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing small caliber ammunition.

Name: Tank Ammunition Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing ammunition for use in tanks.

Name: Weapons Industry
Description: Facilities manufacturing military weapons.  Does not include those manufacturing weapons intended solely for civilian use.

Name: Cannon Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing cannon caliber firearms.

Name: Man-Portable Weapons Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing firearms that can be carried by an individual soldier.

Name: Mounted Weapons Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing firearms designed to be mounted on a vehicle or vessel.

Name: Laser Weapons Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing laser weapons systems.

Name: Nuclear Weapons Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing nuclear weapons systems.

Name: Chemical Weapons Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Facilities manufacturing, maintaining, and repairing chemical weapons systems.
CrosssectorReference: 3.1.6 Potential Chemical Weapon Agents and Precursors

Name: Troop Support Industry
Description: Facilities manufacturing material supporting military personnel.

Name: Chemical Biological Defense System Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing equipment to protect troops against chemical and biological agents.

Name: Clothing and Textile Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing military uniforms, boots, gloves, and other clothing.
CrosssectorReference: 18.5 Textile, Textile Product, Apparel, Leather Manufacturing

Name: Soldier System Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing equipment and gear (other than firearms and munitions) carried by military personnel (e.g., helmets, goggles, knives).

Name: Subsistence and Medical Equipment Manufacturing
Description: Facilities manufacturing food (e.g., Meals-Ready-to-Eat [MRE], mess hall supplies, bottled water, drinks) and medical supplies and equipment (e.g., individual field medical supplies, field hospital supplies).

Name: Information Technology Industry
Description: Establishments manufacturing military-use information technology.

Name: Hardware Manufacturing and Maintenance
Description: Assets that support the production, maintenance, and repair of hardware computing equipment, such as computer peripherals, networking equipment, personal computers, and similar electronic products, or that manufacture components for such products.

Name: Network Development and Maintenance
Description: Assets that support the production, maintenance, and repair of network access and communication devices, routing and switching equipment, and wireless networking products.

Name: Security
Description: Facilities engaged in manufacturing military electronic equipment and developing software used to secure information.

Name: Software Production
Description: Assets that support the production of software, such as operating systems, utilities software, wireless software, or that produce components for such products.

Name: Trainer and Simulator
Description: Facilities engaged in developing and deploying military training equipment and simulators.

Name: Electronics Industry
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of electronics specifically aimed at military applications. Does not include facilities manufacturing equipment solely for civilian use.

Name: Avionics Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of electronic equipment for use primarily in aircraft.

Name: Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (C4ISR) Systems Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C4ISR) systems.

Name: Electronics Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of electronic equipment.

Name: Sensor Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of sensors and detectors.

Name: Electrical Industry Commodity Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of electrical equipment commodities (other than those itemized above). Includes facilities manufacturing equipment for both military and civilian use.

Name: Auxiliary Power Unit Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of auxiliary power units used primarily in aircraft.

Name: Battery Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of batteries.

Name: Electric Motor Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of electric motors.

Name: Electric Generator Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of electric generators.

Name: Power Source Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of power sources (other than batteries and electric generators).

Name: Electronic Industry Commodity Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of electronic commodities. Includes facilities manufacturing equipment for both military and civilian use.

Name: Circuit Board Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of circuit boards.

Name: Global Positioning System (GPS) Receiver Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of Global Positioning System (GPS) receivers.

Name: Guidance/Control Equipment Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of guidance and control equipment.

Name: Microwave Device Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of microwave devices.

Name: Optics Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of optical instruments and lenses.

Name: Semiconductor Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of semiconductors.

Name: Mechanical Industry Commodity Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of mechanical commodities. Includes facilities manufacturing equipment for both military and civilian use.

Name: Aircraft Transmission Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of aircraft transmissions.

Name: Automotive Transmission Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of automotive transmissions.

Name: Bearings Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of bearings.

Name: Diesel Engine Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of diesel engines.

Name: Hydraulic Equipment Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of hydraulic equipment.

Name: Landing Gear Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of aircraft landing gear.

Name: Nuclear Components Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of nuclear components.

Name: Pump and Compressor Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in pump and compressor manufacturing.

Name: Rocket Engine Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of rocket engines.

Name: Turbine Engine Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of turbine engines.

Name: Structural Industry Commodity Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of mechanical commodities. Includes facilities manufacturing equipment for both military and civilian use.

Name: Casting Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in pouring molten metal into molds or dies to form castings.

Name: Ceramic Armor Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of ceramic armor.

Name: Composite Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of composites.

Name: Depleted Uranium Armor Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of depleted uranium armor.

Name: Forging Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in the manufacturing of forgings.

Name: Precious Metal Product Manufacturing
Description: Establishments engaged in manufacturing products using precious metals.

Name: Research & Development Facility
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development). These industries are defined on the basis of the domain of research or the scientific expertise of the establishment. This sub-sector includes those enterprises that are focused primarily on the Defense Industrial Base.

Name: Shipbuilding
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the shipbuilding industry.

Name: Aircraft
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the aircraft industry.

Name: Missile
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the missile industry.

Name: Space
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the space industry.

Name: Combat Vehicle Industry
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the combat vehicle industry.

Name: Ammunition Industry
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the ammunition industry.

Name: Weapon Industry
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the weapons industry.

Name: Troop Support Industry
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the troop support industry.

Name: Information Technology Industry
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the information technology industry.

Name: Electronics Industry
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigation undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the electronics industry.

Name: Electrical Industry Commodity
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the electrical industry.

Name: Mechanical Industry Commodity
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the mechanical industry.

Name: Structural Industry Commodity
Description: Establishments engaged in conducting original investigations undertaken on a systematic basis to gain new knowledge (research) and/or the application of research findings or other scientific knowledge for the creation of new or significantly improved products or processes (experimental development) in the structural industry.

Name: Energy
Description: The Energy Sector is comprised of assets that relate to producing or supplying energy. This includes assets and facilities that produce or supply electricity, petroleum, natural gas, ethanol, biodiesel, hydrogen, coal, and renewable energies.

Name: Electricity
Description: Facilities involved in the generation, transmission, distribution, marketing, and regulation of electricity.

Name: Electricity Generation
Description: Facilities that convert other forms of energy into electricity. Other forms of energy converted include: water (e.g., hydroelectric), fossil fuels, uranium, wind, geothermal, and solar.

Name: Hydroelectric Power Generation
Description: Facilities using water power to drive a turbine and produce electricity.

Name: Hydroelectric Dam
Description: Facilities with a dam and a water storage reservoir where water is released at controlled rates through the turbines. The dams are categorized as high-, medium-, or low-head depending on their height.

Name: Pumped Storage Facility
Description: Facilities with a dam and a water storage reservoir where water is pumped up behind the dam during low electricity demand periods. It is released to run through the turbines to generate electricity during peak load periods.

Name: Run-of-river Generator
Description: Facilities without a dam, or only a relatively small one, and rely on the continuous flow of a river to operate the turbines.

Name: Renewable Energy Generation
Description: Renewable energy source facilities, other than hydroelectric power, used to generate electricity.

Name: Wind
Description: Towers and turbines that are turned by wind to produce electricity.

Name: Solar
Description: Solar collectors and associated equipment designed to capture the sun's light and heat and convert it into electricity.

Name: Geothermal
Description: Equipment and associated systems designed to capture heat from the earth and convert it into electricity.

Name: Fossil Fuel Electric Power Generation
Description: Facilities that use fossil fuels, such as coal, oil, or natural gas, in steam turbine, internal combustion, or combustion turbine processes to produce electricity.

Name: Coal-fired Generator
Description: Facilities that use coal as their fuel source.

Name: Natural Gas-fired Generator
Description: Facilities that use natural gas as their fuel source.

Name: Oil-Fired Generator
Description: Facilities that use oil (e.g., residual fuel oil, diesel fuel) as their fuel source.

Name: Nuclear Electric Power Generation
Description: Facilities using nuclear power to produce electricity for use in the electric power grid.

Name: Light Water Reactor Power Plant
Description: Nuclear power plants with pressurized water reactors or boiling water reactors. Includes all equipment (e.g., steam generators, turbines, generators, transformers, switchgear) used to provide electricity to the grid.
CrosssectorReference:
17.1.1.1 Boiling Water Reactor
17.1.1.2 Pressurized Water Reactor

Name: Other Reactor Power Plant
Description: Nuclear power plants cooled by other than light water. There are no facilities of this type currently operating in the U.S., although there are several in Canada and other countries. Includes all equipment (e.g., steam generators, turbines, generators, transformers, switchgear) used to provide electricity to the grid.

Name: Other Electric Power Generation
Description: Facilities that convert other forms of energy into electricity.

Name: Electricity Transmission
Description: Facilities used to transmit bulk quantities of electricity over relatively long distances using high voltages (usually > 69 kV).

Name: Transmission Line
Description: Transmission towers and lines.

Name: Transmission Substation
Description: Connection points in the transmission network and may include transformers, switches, reactors, circuit breakers, series or shunt capacitors, phase shifters, and switchgear.

Name: Direct Current (DC) Converter Station
Description: Facilities that convert high voltage power between alternating current (AC) and direct current (DC) for improved efficiency in transmission.

Name: Generation Dispatch and Transmission Control Center
Description: Centers that control the dispatching of generators and the high voltage transmission system. Includes generation and transmission supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems. Includes independent system operator (ISO) facilities.

Name: Electricity Distribution
Description: Facilities used to transmit electricity to final consumers at lower voltages (usually <69 kV).

Name: Distribution Line
Description: Facilities that include lines, poles, transformers, and meters.

Name: Distribution Substation
Description: Connection points in the distribution system and from the distribution system to the transmission system. May include transformers, capacitors, and switchgear.

Name: Distribution Control and Dispatch Center
Description: Centers that control the lower voltage distribution system. Includes distribution supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems.

Name: Electricity Market
Description: Business operations that deal in the sale and purchase of electricity on the wholesale level.

Name: Generation Market
Description: Markets into which generation companies bid capacity.  May be operated by an independent system operator (ISO).

Name: Transmission Market
Description: Markets for transmission capacity. May be operated by an independent system operator (ISO) or a transmission company. Includes open access same-time information systems (OASIS).

Name: Electric Vehicle Charging Station
Description: Facility where plug-in electric vehicles can charge their batteries.

Name: Electric Vehicle Rapid-charging Station
Description: Facility where plug-in electric vehicles can have their batteries charged up to at least one half capacity in 30 minutes or less.

Name: Electric Vehicle Standard-charging Station
Description: Facility where plug-in electric vehicles can have their batteries fully charged in four to eight hours.

Name: Other Electricity Facility
Description: Facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Petroleum
Description: Facilities involved in the exploration, supply, processing, storage, transport, distribution, marketing, and regulation of petroleum. Includes crude oil and refined petroleum products.

Name: Crude Oil Supply
Description: These facilities are involved in supplying crude oil.

Name: Drilling Rig
Description: Drilling, monitoring, and testing equipment that creates long-lasting stable boreholes into the ground or sea bottom and connects to an oil and gas reservoir so that a producing well can be established.  The drilling equipment samples soil and rock so that an appropriate and sustainable borehole can be encased and protected from changes in pressure and the environment to allow the borehole to safely produce oil and/or gas for many decades.

Name: On-Shore Well
Description: Land-based facilities used for the extraction of crude oil from the ground. A number of wells may be grouped closely together to extract oil from a reservoir. Includes equipment such as gathering lines used to bring the output of several wells to a central point, storage tanks, and other equipment. Note: Natural gas is sometimes extracted from the same wells that are used to extract oil. This is referred to as "associated gas."

Name: Off-Shore Well
Description: Sea-based facilities used for the extraction of crude oil. Includes equipment such as gathering lines used to bring the output on shore. Note: Natural gas is sometimes extracted from the same wells that are used to extract oil. This is referred to as "associated gas."
CrosssectorReference: 11.4.2.2.9 Outer Continental Shelf Crude Oil Platform

Name: Crude Oil Production from Other Sources
Description: Facilities designed to extract crude oil from other sources.

Name: Gas-Oil Separation Plant
Description: Facilities designed to separate gases and contaminants from the crude oil.

Name: Oil Shale Production
Description: Equipment and facilities used to produce oil from oil shale.  This usually includes mining equipment to extract the oil shale, retort equipment to heat the oil shale into a liquid, and an upgrade facility to produce the synthetic crude oil from the oil shale.  Where in situ processing occurs, the oil shale is not mined, but drilling equipment is required to place heaters in the oil shale to heat it in place. Drilling equipment is then required to create boreholes into (the now) liquid reservoirs and to put wells in place for production.

Name: Crude Oil Storage
Description: Facilities for storing crude oil other than storage at refineries.

Name: Strategic Petroleum Reserve
Description: The Strategic Petroleum Reserve is a U.S. Government complex of four sites created in deep underground salt caverns along the Texas and Louisiana Gulf Coast that stores+H62 emergency supplies of crude oil.

Name: Crude Oil Bulk Storage
Description: Facilities used for the storage and/or marketing of crude oil. Includes storage tanks, pipes and pumps, control machinery, and other equipment. Does not include storage at refineries.

Name: Crude Oil Land-based Terminal
Description: Land-based terminals for storing crude oil. Generally receives and distributes crude oil via pipeline from either domestic or import suppliers. Includes the valves, piping, control machinery, and other equipment to connect to the pipelines serving it.

Name: Crude Oil Marine Terminal
Description: Terminals located on a waterway. Generally receives and distributes crude oil via pipeline, barge, or marine tanker from either domestic or import suppliers. Includes storage tanks, piping, control machinery, and other equipment.
CrosssectorReference: 11.4.2.2.4 Deep Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal - Crude/Product

Name: Crude Oil Transport
Description: Facilities for the transport of crude oil.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline
Description: Pipeline facilities for the transport of crude oil.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline Segment
Description: Components of pipelines such as interconnections and valves.  Includes above ground, under ground, river crossings, and other segments.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline Pumping Station
Description: Stations along the length of a pipeline. Includes pumps, valves, control machinery and breakout storage.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate a pipeline(s). Includes supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system control centers.

Name: Crude Oil Marine Transport
Description: Marine facilities and assets for transporting crude oil.

Name: Crude Oil Tanker
Description: Marine vessels for moving crude oil. Includes crude carriers for importing crude oil, tankers and barges for movement of crude oil on coastal and inland waterways.

Name: Port Handling Crude Oil
Description: Port facilities for mooring, docking, loading, and unloading marine vessels with crude oil. Includes shuttle tankers to off-load larger vessels. Note: Port may also handle other marine cargo.
CrosssectorReference:
11.4.2.1.3 Shallow Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal
11.4.2.2.4 Deep Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal - Crude/Product

Name: Petroleum Processing
Description: Facilities involved in producing petroleum products from crude oil.

Name: Petroleum Refinery
Description: Facilities that manufacture petroleum products (e.g., gasoline and blending components, diesel fuel, jet fuel) from crude oil, natural gas liquids, or other sources. Includes on-site storage.

Name: Petroleum Product Storage
Description: Facilities for storing petroleum products. Does not include storage at refineries, in production fields, and breakout storage along pipeline routes.

Name: Petroleum Product Bulk Storage
Description: Facilities used for the storage and/or marketing of petroleum products. Includes storage tanks, pipes and pumps, control machinery, and other equipment. These facilities are not used to store petroleum products for final consumption. Does not include storage at refineries. Also referred to as tank farms.

Name: Petroleum Product Land-based Bulk Terminal
Description: Large land-based terminals (generally >50,000 barrels storage capacity). Generally receives and distributes its petroleum products via pipeline from either domestic or import suppliers. Includes the valves, piping, control machinery, and other equipment to connect to the pipelines serving it.

Name: Petroleum Product Marine Bulk Terminal
Description: Large terminal (generally >50,000 barrels storage capacity) located on a waterway. Generally receives and distributes its petroleum via pipeline, barge, or marine tanker from either domestic or foreign suppliers.
CrosssectorReference:
11.4.2.1.3 Shallow Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal 
11.4.2.2.4 Deep Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal - Crude/Product

Name: Petroleum Product Bulk Plant
Description: Smaller facilities (generally <50,000 barrels storage capacity) used for the storage and/or marketing of petroleum products. Includes storage tanks, pipes and pumps, control machinery, and other equipment. If distributing gasoline, also includes terminal rack facilities for tanker trucks and will usually have ethanol blending facilities as well. May receive fuel by pipeline, vessel, rail car, or tanker truck; usually distributes to customers via tanker truck. Also referred to as bulk stations.

Name: Underground Petroleum Gas Storage
Description: Underground facilities (e.g., salt caverns) used to store large quantities of petroleum gases (e.g., liquefied petroleum gas [LPG]). Note: Does not include underground storage of natural gas, which is categorized elsewhere.

Name: Petroleum Product Storage for Retail Distribution
Description: Facilities used to store petroleum products for retail distribution to end-use customers.

Name: Motor Vehicle Fueling Station
Description: Facilities dispensing motor vehicle fuels (e.g., gasoline, diesel, gasohol) to consumers. May include maintenance and repair facilities.

Name: Heating Oil Dealer
Description: Facilities for retailing heating oil to consumers with deliveries made by truck.

Name: Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) Dealer
Description: Facilities for retailing liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). Deliveries generally made by truck or in consumer-sized tanks.

Name: End-User Petroleum Product Storage
Description: Facilities at end-user sites (e.g., factories, airports) used for the storage of petroleum products.

Name: Non-Military End-User Petroleum Product Storage
Description: Petroleum product storage facilities for non-military users including agriculture, industry, electric utilities, commercial, and federal, state, and local government facilities.

Name: Military End-User Petroleum Product Storage
Description: Storage facilities for military use of petroleum products.

Name: Petroleum Product Transport
Description: Facilities for the transport of crude oil and petroleum products.

Name: Petroleum Product Pipeline
Description: Pipeline facilities for the transport of petroleum products.

Name: Petroleum Product Pipeline Segment
Description: Lengths of pipeline, interconnections, valves. Includes above ground, underground, river crossings, and other segments.
CrosssectorReference: 11.6.2.1 Petroleum Product Pipeline Component or Interconnect

Name: Petroleum Product Pipeline Pumping Station
Description: Stations along the length of a pipeline. Includes pumps, valves, control machinery, and breakout storage.

Name: Petroleum Product Pipeline Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate a pipeline(s). Includes supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system control centers.

Name: Petroleum Product Marine Transport
Description: Marine facilities for transporting petroleum products.

Name: Petroleum Product Tanker
Description: Marine vessels for moving petroleum products. Includes ocean-going tankers for movement of imported and domestic petroleum products, tankers, and barges for movement of petroleum products on coastal and inland waterways.

Name: Ports Handling Petroleum Product
Description: Port facilities for mooring, docking, loading, and unloading marine vessels with petroleum products. Includes shuttle tankers to off-load larger vessels. Note: Port may also handle other marine cargo.
CrosssectorReference:
11.4.2.1.3 Shallow Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal 
11.4.2.2.4 Deep Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal - Crude/Product

Name: Petroleum Product Rail Transport
Description: Railroad assets for transporting petroleum products.

Name: Railroad Petroleum Tank Car
Description: Railroad tank cars designed for carrying petroleum products. Includes railroad tank cars used for mobile storage (i.e., the product is not off-loaded into a stationary tank but is used directly from the tank car).

Name: Petroleum Product Road Transport
Description: Road assets for transporting petroleum products.

Name: Tanker Truck
Description: Trucks (generally semi-trailers) designed to carry petroleum products.

Name: Tanker Truck Loading Rack
Description: Facilities at some storage facilities and refineries that are used to load tanker trucks that deliver petroleum products.

Name: Petroleum Supporting Facility
Description: Facilities providing support functions in the petroleum system.

Name: Crude Oil Market
Description:
Business operations that deal in the sale and purchase of crude oil and crude oil futures.  Includes centralized exchanges (e.g., New York Mercantile Exchange
[NYMEX]) and trading houses.

Name: Petroleum Product Market
Description:
Business operations that deal in the sale and purchase of petroleum and petroleum futures.  Includes centralized exchanges (e.g., New York Mercantile Exchange
 [NYMEX]) and trading houses.

Name: Natural Gas
Description: Facilities involved in the supply, processing, storage, transport, distribution, marketing, and regulation of natural gas.

Name: Natural Gas Supply
Description: Facilities involved in supplying natural gas.

Name: Drilling Rig
Description: Drilling, monitoring, and testing equipment that creates long-lasting stable boreholes into the ground or sea bottom that connect to an oil and gas reservoir so that a producing well can be put in place. The drilling equipment samples soil and rock so that an appropriate and sustainable borehole can be encased and protected from changes in pressure and the environment to allow the borehole to safely produce oil and/or gas for many decades.

Name: On-Shore Well
Description: Land-based facilities used for the extraction of natural gas from the ground. A number of wells may be grouped closely together to extract gas from a reservoir. Includes equipment such as gathering lines used to bring the output of several wells to a central point. Note: Natural gas is sometimes extracted from the same wells that are used to extract oil. This is referred to as "associated gas." Natural gas that is produced from wells that do not produce oil is referred to as "non-associated gas."

Name: Off-Shore Well
Description: Sea-based facilities used for the extraction of natural gas. Includes equipment such as gathering lines used to bring the output on shore. Also referred to as drilling platforms or off-shore rigs.
CrosssectorReference: 11.4.2.2.10 Outer Continental Shelf Natural Gas Platform

Name: Unconventional Gas Extraction Facility
Description: Unconventional gas extraction facilities used to reach gas that is difficult and/or expensive to exploit.  Gas extraction from coalbeds, tight formations, and shale are considered unconventional and require additional equipment to pump water, drill horizontally, and use water and chemicals to fracture the formations and release the gas.

Name: Natural Gas Production from Other Sources
Description: Facilities designed to produce natural gas from other sources.

Name: Natural Gas Processing
Description: Facilities that process natural gas.

Name: Natural Gas Processing Plant
Description: Facilities that separate liquid hydrocarbons (other than crude oil and propane) from extracted natural gas, remove moisture and other impurities from gas, and/or add odorants to natural gas.

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Plant
Description: Facilities involved in processing liquefied natural gas (LNG).

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Liquefaction Plant
Description: Facilities that liquefy natural gas by reducing its temperature to very low levels (-263° F) at atmospheric pressure. Includes specially designed tanks to store the liquefied natural gas (LNG). May include facilities to regasify LNG for injection into pipelines.

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Satellite Storage and Regasification Plant
Description: Facilities that store liquefied natural gas (LNG) and regasify it for injection into pipelines. Includes specially designed tanks to store the LNG.

Name: Natural Gas Storage
Description: Facilities for storing natural gas.
CrosssectorReference:
11.6.3.4 Natural Gas Transmission Storage
11.6.4.4 Natural Gas Distribution Storage

Name: Underground Storage Facility
Description: Natural gas that is stored underground in aquifers, depleted oil and gas reservoirs, salt caverns, and other geologic formations. Includes surface facilities to inject, monitor, and extract gas. These facilities are typically used to meet peak load gas demands. Note: Does not include liquefied natural gas (LNG), compressed natural gas (CNG), or liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) storage facilities.

Name: Above Ground Storage Facility
Description: Natural gas that is stored in tanks above ground. Includes facilities to inject, monitor, and withdraw gas. Note: Does not include liquefied natural gas (LNG), compressed natural gas (CNG), or liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) storage facilities.

Name: Propane Peak Shaving Facility
Description: Facilities used by natural gas supplies to store, process, and deliver propane into the natural gas distribution system for peak gas demands.

Name: Natural Gas Transport
Description: Facilities for moving large volumes of natural gas over long distances.

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline
Description: Large, high-volume pipelines.

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Segment
Description: Lengths of pipeline, interconnections, and valves. Includes above ground, under ground, river crossings, and other segments.
CrosssectorReference: 11.6.3.1 Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Component or Interconnect

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Compressor Station
Description: Stations along the length of a transmission pipeline. Includes gas-powered or electric compressors, valves, control systems, and associated equipment.
CrosssectorReference: 11.6.3.2 Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Compressor Station

Name: Natural Gas Pipeline Interconnect
Description: Facilities that link one company to another company to transfer gas custody or provide emergency transportation service between companies. This includes facilities such as pipeline segments, valves, or metering and/or pressure reduction stations.
CrosssectorReference: 11.6.3.1 Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Component or Interconnect

Name: Natural Gas Pipeline Hub
Description: A market or supply area for pooling and delivery of gas where transactions occur to facilitate the movement of gas between and among interstate pipelines. Transactions can include a change in title of gas ownership, a change in gas transporter, aggregation of gas supply, or other similar items.

Name: Natural Gas Receipt/Delivery Metering Station
Description: Gas custody transfer metering stations along transmission pipelines. Used to monitor the amount of gas that is transported and provide quantity measurements for billing purposes.

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate a transmission pipeline(s). Generally includes supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system control equipment.
CrosssectorReference: 11.6.3.3 Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Control Center

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Transport
Description: Facilities that transport liquefied natural gas (LNG).

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Tanker
Description: Specially-designed ships for carrying liquefied natural gas (LNG) and maintaining very low temperatures. Generally used for imported LNG.

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Port
Description: Port facilities designed to handle liquefied natural gas (LNG) tankers. Includes mooring facilities, loading and unloading facilities. Includes specially designed storage tanks. Includes regasification equipment to regasify LNG for injection into pipelines.

Name: Natural Gas Distribution
Description: Facilities, generally owned by local distribution companies (LDCs), to distribute natural gas to final consumers.

Name: City Gate Station
Description: Measuring, custody transfer, and pressure regulating stations where a natural gas distribution company receives gas from a transmission company and where pressure is reduced and odorant is added to meet distribution network requirements.

Name: Natural Gas Distribution Pipeline Network
Description: The network of lower pressure pipelines that provide natural gas to consumers.

Name: Natural Gas Distribution Control and Dispatch Center
Description: These centers control the lower pressure gas distribution system. Includes distribution supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems.
CrosssectorReference: 11.6.4.3 Natural Gas Distribution Control or Dispatch Center

Name: Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) Plant
Description: Facilities that compress natural gas to very high pressures (2,000-5,000 psi) Includes specially designed tanks to store the gas. May include facilities to dispense the gas to smaller tanks (e.g., compressed natural gas [CNG] fueled vehicles).

Name: Natural Gas Supporting Facility
Description: Facilities providing support functions in the natural gas system.

Name: Natural Gas Market
Description: Business operations that deal in the sale and purchase of natural gas on the wholesale level.

Name: Other Natural Gas Facility
Description: Other natural gas supporting facilities not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Coal
Description: Facilities involved in the supply, processing, storage, transport, distribution, marketing, and regulation of coal.

Name: Coal Supply
Description: Facilities involved in supplying coal.

Name: Underground Mine
Description: Coal mines where the coal seam is underground and is accessed through mine shafts.

Name: Surface Mine
Description: Coal mines where the coal seam is close to the surface and is mined by stripping away top layers of soil (referred to as overburden). Also called "strip mines" or "open pit mines."

Name: Coal Processing
Description: Facilities that process coal.

Name: Coal Preparation Plant (Coal Cleaning Plant)
Description: Facilities that process coal to remove mineral impurities, crush and grind to create more uniform size distribution, and/or reduce sulfur content.

Name: Coal Gasification Plant
Description: Facilities that further process coal, after the preparation plant, to produce synthetic natural gas.

Name: Coal Liquefaction Plant
Description: Facilities that further process coal, after the preparation plant, to produce synthetic liquid fuels (e.g., gasoline, diesel fuel).

Name: Coal Transport
Description: Facilities for moving large volumes of coal to end users.

Name: Coal Rail Transport
Description: Rail facilities for transport of coal.

Name: Coal Unit Train Route
Description: Rail lines used by unit trains that haul only coal.

Name: Other Coal Train Route
Description: Rail lines used by trains that haul coal and other commodities.

Name: Coal Rail Car
Description: Hopper or gondola rail cars dedicated to the movement of coal.

Name: Coal Water Transport
Description: Water facilities for transport of coal by ship or barge.

Name: Coal Water Transport Route
Description: Routes for movement of large quantities of coal.

Name: Coal Port
Description: Port facilities for handling large quantities of coal.
CrosssectorReference:
11.4.2.1.2 Shallow Draft Dry Bulk Cargo Terminal
11.4.2.2.3 Deep Draft Dry Bulk Cargo Terminal

Name: Coal Barge
Description: Long flat-bottomed boats (that usually cannot move under its own power) used to transport large quantities of coal on inland waterways.

Name: Coal Supporting Facility
Description: Facilities that provide support functions in the coal system.

Name: Coal Market
Description: Business operations that deal in the sale and purchase of coal and coal futures, derivatives, and options. Includes brokers using centralized exchanges (e.g., New York Mercantile Exchange [NYMEX]) and Over the Counter (OTC) deals.

Name: Other Coal Facility
Description: Coal facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Ethanol
Description: Facilities involved in the supply, processing, storage, transport, distribution, marketing, and regulation of ethanol.

Name: Ethanol Feed Material Supply
Description: Facilities involved in supplying feed material (e.g., corn, sugar cane, other biomass) for the production of ethanol.

Name: Ethanol Production Facility
Description: Facilities involved in the production of ethanol.

Name: Ethanol Storage Facility
Description: Facilities for storing ethanol which include tanks at ethanol plants or rail cars and storage tanks at bulk terminals storing gasoline.

Name: Ethanol Transport
Description: Facilities for transporting ethanol primarily rail cars.

Name: Ethanol Unit Train Route
Description: Train routes for ethanol unit trains.

Name: Ethanol Non-Unit Train
Description: Train routes for non-unit train ethanol rail movements.

Name: Ethanol Rail Car
Description: Rail cars dedicated to the movement of ethanol.

Name: Ethanol Blending Facility
Description: Facilities involved in blending ethanol with gasoline. Frequently done at terminals where tanker trucks are loaded for delivery to motor vehicle fueling stations.

Name: Ethanol Distribution Facility
Description: Facilities made up of storage tanks and fuel dispensing equipment to fuel ethanol vehicles.

Name: E-85 Fueling Station
Description: Fuel dispensing equipment to fill vehicles with E85.

Name: Ethanol Market
Description: Markets trading in ethanol.

Name: Other Ethanol Supporting Facility
Description: Ethanol facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Biodiesel
Description: Facilities involved in the supply, processing, storage, transport, distribution, marketing, and regulation of biodiesel, a fuel typically made from soybean, canola, or other vegetable oils, animal fats, and recycled grease. It can serve as a substitute for petroleum-derived diesel or distillate fuel.

Name: Biodiesel Feed Material Supply
Description: Facilities involved in supplying feed material, organic non-fossil material of biological origin that is usually a byproduct or a discarded product. Biodiesel feedstock includes municipal solid waste from biogenic sources, landfill gas, sludge waste, agricultural crops and byproducts, straw and other biomass solids, liquids, and gases; can include recycled cooking oil.

Name: Biodiesel Production Facility
Description: Assets that manufacture biodiesel from organic non-fossil material, usually manufactured from vegetable oils, animal fats, and recycled cooking oils.

Name: Biodiesel Storage Facility
Description: Facilities with tanks to store and/or market biodiesel. Includes storage tanks, pipes and pumps, control machinery, and other equipment.

Name: Biodiesel Transport Facility
Description: Transport facilities that are either at the biorefinery where the biodiesel is made or at the terminal where it will be delivered to the customer. Movement from production facility to storage tanks or to end user is usually done via a tanker truck.

Name: Biodiesel Distribution Facility
Description: Facilities made up of storage tanks and fuel dispensing equipment to fuel biodiesel vehicles.

Name: Biodiesel Fueling Station
Description: Fuel dispensing equipment to fuel biodiesel vehicles. Often customers use a blend of biodiesel with regular diesel. May have diesel storage on site and blending equipment to make an appropriate biodiesel blend.

Name: Other Biodiesel Supporting Facility
Description: Biodiesel facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Hydrogen
Description: Facilities associated with hydrogen, the lightest of all gases, occurring chiefly in combination with oxygen in water. Hydrogen exists also in acids, bases, alcohols, petroleum, and other hydrocarbons; it is an energy carrier not an energy source.

Name: Hydrogen Source Material Supply
Description: Facilities associated with supplying the source material for hydrogen. It is usually produced from hydrocarbons, most frequently natural gas or methane; biomass; or it can be separated from oxygen in water with enough heat, electricity or by using microbes.

Name: Hydrogen Production Facility
Description: Facilities and the associated equipment that produce hydrogen.

Name: Hydrogen Storage Facility
Description: Storage facilities for hydrogen are located at production facilities, refineries, and chemical plants that use hydrogen.

Name: Hydrogen Transport Facility
Description: Facilities with storage and fueling equipment to load trucks carrying compressed hydrogen to end-users.

Name: Hydrogen Distribution Facility
Description: Facilities supporting the fueling of end-user hydrogen vehicles.

Name: Hydrogen Fueling Station
Description: Storage tanks and dispensing equipment to refill hydrogen-fueled vehicles.

Name: Other Hydrogen Supporting Facility
Description: Hydrogen facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Other Energy Conversion System
Description: Energy conversion systems not listed elsewhere.

Name: District Energy System
Description: Systems that produce and distribute electricity, hot water, steam, and chilled water from a central "trigeneration" power/energy plant's location.

Name: District Heating
Description: Facilities or assets associated with steam or hot water from an outside source used as an energy source in a building. The steam or hot water is produced in a central plant and piped into the building. The district heat may be purchased from a utility or provided by a physical plant in a separate building that is part of the same facility (for example, a hospital complex or university).

Name: District Cooling
Description: Facilities or assets associated with chilled water from an outside source used as an energy source in a building; the chilled water is produced in a central plant and piped into the building. The district coolant may be purchased from a utility or provided by a physical plant in a separate building that is part of the same facility (for example, a hospital complex or university).

Name: District Power
Description: Facilities or assets associated with electricity from an outside source used as an energy source in a building. The electricity is produced in a central plant and transmitted to the building. The district electricity may be purchased from a utility or provided by a physical plant in a separate building that is part of the same facility (for example, a hospital complex or university).

Name: Combined Heat and Power
Description: A plant designed to produce both heat and electricity from a single heat source. Note: Combined heat and power is used in place of the U.S. Energy Information Administration term "cogenerator" as this term better describes the facility because some of the plants do not produce heat and power in a sequential fashion and, as a result, do not meet the legal definition of cogeneration specified in the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act (PURPA).

Name: Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
