Description: Organizations that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight and support to the energy system.

Name: Federal Energy Agency
Description: Federal agencies that deal with the energy system e.g., Department of Energy, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, and Department of Transportation.

Name: State, Local, Regional Energy Agency
Description: State, local, and regional agencies regulating energy companies operating in their jurisdiction including public utility commissions, environmental agencies, and others.

Name: Energy Industry Organization
Description: Industry organizations (e.g., National Electric Reliability Council (NERC), American Petroleum Institute, American Gas Association) that provide industry-wide support.

Name: International Energy Organization
Description: International organizations dealing with global energy issues.

Name: Emergency Services
Description: Assets involved in emergency response designed to save lives, protect property and the environment, assist in the management of disasters (both natural and manmade), and aid in recovery.

Name: Law Enforcement
Description: Federal, state, and local law enforcement assets used to protect the public, conduct criminal investigations, gather evidence, and apprehend criminal perpetrators.

Name: Law Enforcement Administrative Office/Headquarters
Description: Central office that manages law enforcement activities in a given jurisdiction.  Note: In many communities, this office is located in a police station.

Name: Law Enforcement Operational Facility
Description: Local facilities, to include police stations, used for the day-to-day law enforcement operations.  Smaller communities may have only one station serving their jurisdiction, while larger communities may have several.

Name: Tactical Unit
Description: Specialized law enforcement units focused on dealing with specific situations (e.g., Special Weapons and Tactics [SWAT] teams, gang crimes units).  Generally have specialized training and equipment (e.g., automatic weapons, night vision equipment).

Name: Special Weapons and Tactics (SWAT) Team
Description: Special Weapons and Tactics (SWAT) teams are specially trained to handle high-risk situations and specialized tactical needs. Team members have advanced skills beyond that of typical patrol officers.

Name: Riot/Crowd Control
Description: Police units trained in handling large crowds and riot situations, including specialized training in crowd dispersal, tactics, and special weapons.

Name: Bomb Squad/Explosives Unit
Description: A public safety agency specializing in the investigation and disarming of suspected explosive devices.

Name: Public Safety Dive Team
Description: Underwater teams used to recover evidence, drowned victims, and lost vessels.

Name: Law Enforcement Patrol Team (Strike Team)
Description: A set number of resources of the same kind and type that have an established minimum number of personnel.

Name: Crime Scene Investigation Unit
Description: Specialized law enforcement units focused on gathering evidence and information at the scene of an incident. Generally have specialized training and equipment (e.g., photographic equipment, fingerprint equipment).

Name: Canine (K-9) Unit
Description: Trained canine units providing law enforcement with a non-lethal means of apprehending dangerous criminal offenders; detecting intruders and alerting handlers to their presence; pursuing, attacking, and holding criminal offenders who resist apprehension; searching and clearing buildings and large open areas for criminals; tracking lost children or other persons; detecting the presence of certain narcotics, explosives, and tobacco products; locating deceased subjects, crime scenes, and minute physical evidence; and providing a strong psychological deterrent to certain types of criminal conduct.

Name: Marine and Port Law Enforcement Unit
Description: Aquatic law enforcement units focused on drug interdiction, smuggling, etc. Includes supporting facilities for vessels (e.g., docks, fueling stations for law enforcement boats).

Name: Aviation Unit
Description: Specialized law enforcement units that use aircraft for law enforcement, surveillance, and search and rescue purposes.

Name: Fixed-Wing Airplane
Description: Fixed-wing aircraft of various sizes used for surveillance, extraditions, personnel, and cargo transportation.

Name: Helicopter
Description: Helicopters of various sizes to provide multifunction aerial support for ground operations.

Name: Search and Rescue Unit
Description: Units Involved in the location, rescue (extrication), and initial medical stabilization of victims trapped in confined spaces.

Name: Other Specialized Law Enforcement Operational Facility
Description: Facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Hazardous Materials (HAZMAT) Response Unit
Description: Units that are trained and equipped to perform work to control actual or potential leaks, spills, discharges, or releases of Hazardous Materials (HAZMAT), requiring possible close approach to the material. The team/equipment may include external or contracted resources.

Name: Correctional Facility
Description: Federal, state, and local facilities for the treatment of offenders through a system of penal incarceration, rehabilitation, probation, and parole, or the administrative system that implements these functions.

Name: Law Enforcement Training Facility
Description: Federal, state, and local facilities that specialize in personnel training programs.

Name: Law Enforcement Maintenance Facility
Description: Federal, state, and local facilities specializing in repair or maintenance of law enforcement vehicles and/or equipment.

Name: Law Enforcement Communication Facility
Description: Facilities where communication equipment (e.g., radios, transmitter and repeater towers, communication centers) are used or stored by law enforcement.  Note: This equipment is frequently shared with other emergency response organizations and government public service agencies.

Name: Mobile Communications Unit
Description: Units that provide on-scene dispatch and radio assistance to law enforcement personnel responding to various incidents.

Name: Law Enforcement Supply Facility
Description: Pre-identified storage and staging areas for law enforcement supplies and equipment.

Name: Private Security Guard Office
Description: Administrative facilities for private security force.

Name: Other Law Enforcement Facility
Description: Facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Fire and Emergency Services
Description: Federal, state, and local facilities and assets used by trained personnel in fire suppression, fire prevention, hazardous materials (HAZMAT) control, emergency rescue, building code enforcement, and public fire safety education. Includes assets used to respond to chemical/biological/radiological incidents.

Name: Fire & Emergency Services Administrative Office/Headquarters
Description: Central offices where fire and hazardous materials (HAZMAT) activities are managed in a given jurisdiction. Note: In many communities these offices are located in a fire station.

Name: Fire & Emergency Services Operational Facility
Description: Local facilities, to include fire stations, used for day-to-day operations and staffed by response personnel.  Includes vehicle bays for fire apparatus and crew sleeping quarters.

Name: Hazardous Materials (HAZMAT) Entry Team
Description: Units specialized in responding to hazardous materials (HAZMAT) emergencies.

Name: Marine Emergency Unit
Description: Aquatic units specialized in emergency operations (e.g., water rescue, firefighting).  Includes supporting facilities for emergency vessels (e.g., docks, fueling stations for fireboats).

Name: Air Emergency Unit
Description: A rotary-wing aircraft configured, staffed, and equipped to respond, care for, and transport patients. A rotary-wing aircraft must be approved/licensed by a State.

Name: Area Command Team, Firefighting
Description: An interagency organization under the auspices of National Wildfire Coordination Group (NWCG) that oversees the management of multiple incidents which are supervised by an incident management team (IMT) organization; or that oversees the management of a very large incident that has multiple IMTs assigned to it. Area Command has the responsibility to set overall strategy and priorities, allocate critical resources based on priorities, ensure incidents are properly managed, objectives are met and strategies followed.

Name: Air Search Team (Fixed-Wing)
Description: Teams that provide airborne search, emergency airlift, airborne communications, and other special services. Varying levels of specialized management support and command and control capabilities are included in team structures.

Name: Air Search and Rescue
Description: Teams that provide search and rescue emergency airlift and other special services at the request of, and to support, state and county agency needs.

Name: Tactical Team
Description: Specialized firefighting units focused on dealing with specific situations (e.g., crew transport, wildland fire suppression).  Tactical units generally have specialized training and equipment that increases the unit’s proficiency in responding to an emergency.

Name: Crew Transport (Firefighting Crew)
Description: Vehicles capable of transporting a specified number of crew personnel in a specified manner.

Name: Hand Crew
Description: Units specialized in combating wildland fires. Hand Crews, teams of multi-skilled professional firefighters, serve as the frontline forces during wildland fire responses.

Name: Search and Rescue Unit
Description: Units specialized in structural collapse, trench, confined space, and rope rescue. Includes response to: caves, collapses, mines and tunnels, mountain areas, radio direction finding, swift water/flood incidents, urban and wilderness areas, and other search and rescue requests.

Name: Other Specialized Fire and Emergency Services Facility
Description: Facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Fire & Emergency Services Apparatus
Description: Fire apparatus and other vehicles used in fire and hazardous materials (HAZMAT) activities. Includes pumpers, ladder trucks, mobile command centers, marine vessels and aircraft.

Name: Helicopter, Firefighting
Description: Aircraft that depend principally on the lift generated by one or more rotors for its support in flight. Capable of the delivery of firefighters, water, or chemical retardants (either a fixed tank or bucket system), and internal or external cargo.

Name: Helitanker, Firefighting
Description: Helicopters equipped with a fixed tank, Air Tanker Board certified, and capable of delivering a minimum of 1,100 gallons of water, retardant, or foam.

Name: Water Tender, Firefighting
Description: Specialized firefighting apparatus designed for transporting water from a water source to a fire scene. Water tenders are capable of drafting water from a stream, lake or hydrant.  Water tenders are used when there is no working fire hydrant within reach of other fire equipment or when they can provide a very rapid connection to the fire engine(s).

Name: Fire Engine
Description: Any ground vehicle that provides specified levels of pumping, water, hose capacity, and staffed with a minimum number of personnel.

Name: Fire Boat
Description: Vessels or watercraft designed and constructed for the purpose of fighting fires providing specified level of pumping capacity. The boat is designed with the ability to carry firefighting foam and personnel to extinguish fires in the marine environment.

Name: Fire Truck, Aerial (Ladder or Platform)
Description: Fire apparatus primarily used for search and rescue, or ventilation tasks.

Name: Foam Tender, Firefighting
Description: Apparatus used to mix concentrate with water to make solution, pump, and mix air and solution to make foam, and transport and apply foam.

Name: Portable Pump
Description: Portable, powered water pumping system designed to use untapped water sources for refilling fire engines.

Name: Brush Patrol Unit
Description: Light, mobile vehicular units with limited pumping and water capacity for off-road operations.

Name: Gas Tanker or Aviation Gas (AvGas) Tanker
Description: Vehicles that carry and dispense gasoline.

Name: Fire & Emergency Services Investigation Laboratory
Description: Facilities equipped to conduct forensic investigations on fires.

Name: Fire Training Facility
Description: Facilities used for fire and hazardous material (HAZMAT) training.  Includes fire academies and training sites.

Name: Fire & Emergency Services Maintenance Facility
Description: Facilities used to maintain equipment and vehicles.

Name: Fire & Emergency Services Communication Facility
Description: Facilities where communication equipment (e.g., radios, transmitter and repeater towers, communication centers) is used or stored by law enforcement.  Note: This equipment is frequently shared with other emergency response organizations and government public service agencies.

Name: Mobile Communications Unit
Description: Vehicle that serves as a self-sustaining mobile operations center capable of operating in an environment with little to no basic services, facilitating communications between multiple entities using an array of fixed and/or wireless communications equipment, providing appropriate work space for routine support functions, and providing basic services for personnel in short-term or long-term deployments.

Name: Fire Alarm System
Description: Equipment used to convey fire alarms to a central dispatching center. Includes alarm equipment on public and private property and central alarm receiving centers.

Name: Fire & Emergency Services Supply Facility
Description: Pre-identified storage and staging areas for fire mitigation supplies and equipment.

Name: Private Fire & Emergency Services Facility
Description: Facilities that house a private company that provides fire response services.

Name: Other Fire & Emergency Services Facility
Description: Other fire and emergency services facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Emergency Medical Services (EMS)
Description: Includes federal, state, and local assets designed to provide medical care at the scene of an incident and during transport of victims to healthcare and public health facilities. Does not include hospital assets, which are categorized elsewhere.

Name: Emergency Medical Services Administration Office/Headquarters
Description: Central offices where Emergency Medical Services (EMS) activities in a jurisdiction are managed. Other functions include billing, report storage, policy development, and administration. Note: In many communities these offices are located in fire operational facilities or fire stations.

Name: Emergency Medical Services Operational Facility
Description: Facilities used for day-to-day Emergency Medical Services (EMS) operations. Includes vehicle bays for EMS apparatus and sleeping quarters for EMS personnel.

Name: Emergency Medical Apparatus
Description: Includes heliports for medivac helicopters and vehicles used in emergency medical activities (e.g., ambulances, mobile intensive care units, medivac helicopters).

Name: Other Specialized Emergency Medical Services
Description: Facilities housing specially trained medical personnel and their equipment (e.g., National Disaster Medical System units, Disaster Medical Assistance Teams, Disaster Mortuary Operational Response Teams).

Name: Emergency Medical Services Training Facility
Description: Facilities used to train Emergency Medical Services (EMS) personnel.

Name: Emergency Medical Services Maintenance Facility
Description: Facilities used to maintain Emergency Medical Services (EMS) equipment and vehicles.

Name: Emergency Medical Services Communication Facility
Description: Facilities where communication equipment (e.g., radios, transmitter and repeater towers, communication centers) is used or stored. Note: This equipment is frequently shared with other emergency response organizations and government public service agencies.

Name: Emergency Medical Services Supply Facility
Description: Pre-identified storage and staging areas for emergency medical supplies and equipment.

Name: Private Emergency Medical Services Facility
Description: Facilities that houses a private company that provides emergency medical services.

Name: Other Emergency Medical Services Facility
Description: Other emergency medical services facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Emergency Management
Description: Assets providing mitigation, prevention, preparedness (including planning, training, and exercising), response (including coordination, resource acquisition, and resource prioritization), recovery efforts, and public information before, during, and after disasters and catastrophic events.

Name: Emergency Management Administrative Office/Headquarters
Description: Central offices where emergency management planning and administrative activities in a jurisdiction are carried out.

Name: Emergency Management Operational Facility
Description: Fixed or mobile assets used to establish and maintain control of an emergency incident.

Name: Public Safety Answering Point
Description: Facilities (e.g., 911 Center) for receiving emergency calls and dispatching responders. Generally handles law enforcement, fire, hazardous material (HAZMAT), and emergency medical operations.

Name: Mutual Aid and Multi-Agency Coordination System (Emergency Operations Center [EOC])
Description: Communication facilities designed to connect agencies in different jurisdictions and allow for coordinated response to emergencies.  May be operated in conjunction with other communication systems.

Name: Mobile Communications Center/Mobile Emergency Operations Center
Description: A vehicle that serves as a self-sustaining mobile operations center capable of operating in an environment with little to no basic services, facilitating communications between multiple entities using an array of fixed and/or wireless communications equipment, providing appropriate work space for routine support functions, and providing basic services for personnel in short-term or long-term deployments.

Name: Mobile Feeding Kitchen/Mobile Field Kitchen
Description: A unit designed and constructed to dispense food for incident personnel providing a specified level of capacity.

Name: Intelligence Fusion Center
Description: Multi-agency collection point for cross-connecting criminal information data. Center (regional or state) for collection and analysis of information and intelligence for homeland security purposes.

Name: Other Specialized Emergency Management Facility
Description: Other emergency medical service facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Emergency Management Training Facility
Description: Facilities used to train emergency management personnel.

Name: Emergency Management Maintenance Facility
Description: Facilities used to maintain emergency management equipment and vehicles.

Name: Emergency Management Communication Facility
Description: Facility where communication equipment (e.g., radios, transmitter and repeater towers, communication centers) is used or stored.  Note: This equipment is frequently shared with other emergency response organizations and government public service agencies.

Name: Emergency Management Information System
Description: Computers, databases, and other information technology used in emergency management.

Name: Emergency Management Supply Facility
Description: Pre-identified storage and staging areas for emergency management supplies and equipment.

Name: Private Emergency Management Facility
Description: Facility that houses a private company providing emergency management services.

Name: Public Emergency Shelter
Description: Facility that provides a refuge for those seeking temporary safety from a disaster or emergency.

Name: Other Emergency Management Facility
Description: Other emergency management facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Public Works
Description: Public works are the physical structures and facilities that are developed or acquired by public agencies to house governmental functions and provide water, power, waste disposal, transportation, and similar services during an incident, emergency, and/or disaster.

Name: Public Works Administration Office/Headquarters
Description: Facilities where the following activities take place: operations management and supervision, financial management and reporting, public relations, procurement of professional services, requesting and evaluating proposals, awarding bids and contract management.

Name: Public Works Operational Facility
Description: Facilities used for day-to-day public works operations. Public works facilities can vary widely but are typically related to urban infrastructure or rural development projects (e.g., water purification plants, sewage treatment facilities).

Name: Public Works Training Facility
Description: Facilities used to train public works personnel.

Name: Public Works Maintenance Facility
Description: Facilities used to maintain public works equipment and vehicles.

Name: Public Works Communication Facility
Description: Facilities where communication equipment (e.g., radios, transmitter and repeater towers, communication centers) is used or stored. Note: This equipment is frequently shared with other emergency response organizations and government public service agencies.

Name: Public Works Supply Facility
Description: Pre-identified storage and staging areas for public works supplies and equipment.

Name: Private Public Works Facility
Description: Facilities that house a private company providing public works services.

Name: Other Public Works Facility
Description: Other public works facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Other Emergency Services Facility
Description: Other emergency services facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Information Technology
Description: The Information Technology (IT) Sector is comprised of physical assets and virtual systems and networks that enable key capabilities and services in both the public and private sectors. Functions are sets of processes that produce, provide, and maintain products and services. These functions encompass the full set of processes involved in creating IT products and services, including R&D, manufacturing, distribution, upgrades, and maintenance. They also support the sector’s ability to produce and provide high-assurance products, services, and practices that are resilient to threats and can be rapidly recovered. Critical IT Sector facilities, operations, and services provide for the design, development, distribution, and support of IT products (hardware and software) and operational support services that are essential or critical to maintain or reconstitute IT Sector critical functions.

Name: Hardware Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of hardware computing equipment, such as computer peripherals, networking equipment, personal computers, and similar electronic products, or that manufacture components for such products.

Name: Network Development
Description: Facilities that support the production of network access and communication devices, security hardware, routing and switching equipment, and wireless networking products.

Name: Semiconductor Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of electronic devices used by or incorporated into other hardware equipment.

Name: Storage Hardware Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of large-scale storage systems, including magnetic and optical data storage systems.

Name: Domain Name System Appliance Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of appliances that support Domain Name System (DNS) functions. Examples include (but are not limited to) servers, network management appliances, and specialized DNS appliances.

Name: Identity Management Technology Production
Description: Facilities that support the incorporation and verification of legitimate individual digital identities to include credential hardware and software and credential verification devices and software.

Name: Control Systems Hardware Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of hardware designed to monitor and control sensitive processes and physical functions. This hardware includes distributed control systems (typically used within a single processing plant or over a small geographic area) and supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems (typically used for large, geographically dispersed systems).

Name: Field Devices Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of units receiving real time process information (e.g., voltage, current, power, pressures, flows, temperatures) and provide control to actuators to change or maintain a process (e.g., breakers, valves, frequency, speed control). These operate with supervisory control and data acquisition Systems (SCADA) and distributed control systems (DCS). Some could stand alone, are network based, and have potential cyber vulnerabilities. Examples of these are the remote terminal units (RTUs), intelligent electronic devices (IEDs), programmable logic controllers  (PLCs), and intelligent sensors.

Name: Other Hardware Production Facility
Description: Hardware production facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Software Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of software, such as operating systems, utilities software, wireless software, or that produce components for such products.

Name: Security Software Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of software for cyber security and policy compliance elements.

Name: Operating System Development
Description: Facilities that support the production of software that communicates with computer hardware. Operating systems perform basic tasks, such as recognizing input from the keyboard, sending output to the display screen, keeping track of files and directories on the disk, and controlling peripheral devices such as disk drives and printers (e.g., Microsoft, Unix, Apple, IBM, supercomputing, render farms, mainframes, servers, workstations, desktops, handheld devices, real-time systems, embedded systems, and components).

Name: Business Software Production
Description: Facilities that support the production of software that manages business productivity, industrial processes, customer care, and retail operations; controls the organization, storage, retrieval, security and integrity of data in a database; and enables analytics, data mining, data warehousing, forecasting, querying, reporting, and data analysis.

Name: Control System Software
Description: Facilities that produce software that supports hardware designed to monitor and control sensitive processes and physical functions. This hardware includes distributed control systems (typically used within a single processing plant or over a small geographic area) and supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems (typically used for large, geographically dispersed systems).

Name: Field Device Software
Description: Facilities that support the production of software that will receive real time process information (e.g., voltage, current, power, pressures, flows, temperatures) and provide control to actuators to change or maintain a process (e.g., breakers, valves, frequency, speed control). This software operates with supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) and distributed control system (DCS) devices. Some devices could stand alone, are network based, and have potential cyber vulnerabilities. Examples of these devices are the remote terminal units (RTUs), intelligent electronic devices (IEDs), programmable logic controllers (PLCs), and intelligent sensors.

Name: Other Software Development Facility
Description: Software production facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Internet-Based Content, Information, and Communications Services
Description: Facilities and establishments engaged in the operation, support, development, and administration of the Internet and its services and products.

Name: Internet Support Service
Description: Facilities that conduct and\or provide internet services and support to preparedness and response, geospatial services and mapping, world wide web, communication and other internet based capabilities.

Name: Fundamental Internet Service
Description: Facilities that support the production of services for Internet access or management and monitoring of Internet access at a high level (e.g., Internet backbone services, Internet performance tracking, global traffic management, Internet Protocol [IP] address assignment, domain name services).

Name: Local Internet Service
Description: Facilities that support the production of Internet access or management and monitoring of Internet access at a low level (e.g. local Internet access, benchmarking services, domain name services).

Name: Internet Licensing Service
Description: Facilities that operate domain registration services, top-level domain (TLD) /root infrastructures, and resolution services.

Name: Internet Routing, Access, and Connection Facility
Description: Facilities that house critical carrier equipment and data servers. Assets that provide and\or support Internet infrastructures, points of presence, peering points, local access services and capabilities.

Name: Other Internet Support or Production Service Facility
Description: Internet-based content, information, and communication services, facilities, and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Other Hardware Production Facility
Description: Facilities that support the production of information technology services.  Includes development, marketing, delivery, and support of information services, managed services, transaction processing, and others.

Name: IT Management Service
Description: Facilities that support outsourced functions for a particular area of Information Technology (IT) on a long-term basis (e.g., managed data center services, managed security services, managed storage services, managed network services, technical support services, application service provider services, collocation services, custom software development, systems integration).

Name: Identity Management and Trust Service
Description: Facilities that provide services, technologies, and infrastructure to ensure the identity, authenticate and authorize entities and ensure confidentiality, integrity, and availability of devices, services, data, and transactions.

Name: IT Quality Assurance Service
Description: Facilities that support steps taken by an organization to ensure that systems are designed, developed, and maintained using formalized and rigorous controls and standards.

Name: Other Operational Support Service Facility
Description: Other operational support service facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Facilities that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight and support to the various components of the Information Technology (IT) system.

Name: Other Information Technology Facility
Description: Information Technology (IT) facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Communications
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in operating, maintaining, and/or providing access to facilities for the transmission of voice, data, text, sound, and video. A communications facility may be based on a single technology or a combination of technologies.

Name: Wired Communication
Description: Establishments engaged in operating and maintaining switching and transmission facilities to provide point-to-point communications via landlines (e.g., copper wire, fiber optic), microwave, or a combination of landlines and satellite linkups or furnishing telegraph and other non-vocal communications using their own facilities. Includes facilities termed the public switched telephone network (PSTN).

Name: Switching Facility
Description: Nodes in the communications network where lines and trunks are connected to form a communication path.

Name: Local Exchange Facility
Description: Switching facilities that serve a local area. Also called end offices or central offices. Lines from homes and businesses terminate in a local exchange facility (end office or central office). These facilities connect customers within the area together. Typically, calls within an exchange area served by these facilities are considered local calls.

Name: Local and Access Tandem Switching Facility
Description: Switching facilities that concentrate and distribute call traffic. They generally connect higher volume trunk lines to each other. Local tandem switches generally connect trunk lines from one local exchange to another within a metropolitan area. Access tandem switches generally connect trunk lines from local exchange facilities into the long distance network.

Name: Inter-Exchange Switching Facility
Description: Switching facilities that handle long distance traffic. Sometimes referred to as "toll" switches. A facility may house the switches owned by competing telephone service providers that have a "point-of-presence" at the facility.

Name: Signaling Network Facility
Description: Facilities that carry network signaling information ( e.g., address, supervision, switching information) that is used to handle call traffic. These facilities may be co-located with or be separate from switching facilities.

Name: Signaling System (SS7) Center
Description: Facilities where signaling equipment is installed and operated.

Name: Signaling Link
Description: Lines or other connection routes that carry network signaling information.

Name: Transport Facility
Description: Links in the communications network that carry call information. Includes copper wire, fiber optic, microwave and other technologies. Includes high volume trunk lines and individual customer lines.

Name: Trunk Line Link
Description: Communication lines between switching facilities. Generally carry large volume of call traffic from many users simultaneously. The links Include physical lines (e.g., copper wire, fiber optic), microwave connections, and other transmission technologies.

Name: Submarine Cable
Description: Submarine cable trunk lines laid in the ocean and across lakes, bays, and rivers to connect across waterways. Also includes cable landing stations where the lines come ashore and are connected to the telephone network.

Name: Customer Line
Description: Communication lines linking end use customers to a switching facility.

Name: Operations Management
Description: Facilities where operational activities and procedures take place to address critical infrastructure coordination efforts toward improving the physical and cyber security protection of Communications Sector assets.  Includes capabilities to ease the flow of information within the sector, across sectors and with designated federal agencies; includes addressing issues related to response and recovery under all hazards to assure the continued operation of vital communications services.

Name: Network Operations Center
Description: Central facilities from which large portions of the telephone network are monitored and managed. In general, each communications company has its own network operations center. Larger companies may have more than one center, including backup facilities.

Name: Call Center/Customer Service Center
Description: Central location where telephone agents, or operators, make outgoing calls or field inbound calls for a company or organization.

Name: Billing Center
Description: Central location that collects invoice information from clients and generates bills on their behalf.

Name: Emergency Operations Center
Description: Central command and control facilities responsible for carrying out the principles of emergency preparedness and emergency management, or disaster management functions at a strategic level in an emergency situation, and ensuring the continuity of operation of a organization or company.

Name: Data Center
Description: Departments that house the computer systems and related equipment, including the data library. Data entry and systems programming may also come under its jurisdiction. A control section is usually provided that accepts work from and releases output to user departments.

Name: Wired Telecommunication Service Provider Facility
Description: Administrative facilities of companies that own and operate wired communications networks.

Name: Access Infrastructure
Description: Gateways for communication between distributed platforms providing clients access to information or resources.

Name: End Office
Description: Telephone central offices that connect directly to the customer.

Name: Internet Service Provider Point of Presence
Description: Sites where there exists a collection of telecommunications equipment, usually modems, digital leased lines and multi-protocol routers. Internet service providers may operate several points of presence (PoPs) distributed throughout their area of operation to increase the chance that their subscribers will be able to reach one with a local telephone call. The alternative is for them to use virtual PoPs via a third party vendor.

Name: Access Tandem
Description: Switches that provide the connection between end offices and the points of presence (PoPs) for interexchange carriers.

Name: Local Loop or Metro Ring
Description: The lines between a customer and the telephone company's central office, often called the "last mile." Local loops use copper-based telephone wire.

Name: Wireline Communications Customer Hosting Center Facility
Description: Telecommunications carrier locations for providing wireline communications services to customers such as local and long distance service, voice over Internet protocol (VOIP), ethernet and managed internet services.

Name: Cable
Description: Two or more wires running side by side and bonded, twisted or braided together to form a single assembly.

Name: Business/Operations Office
Description: Central offices where day-to-day business/operations are conducted.

Name: Headend
Description: Control centers of a cable-television system, where incoming signals are amplified, converted, processed, and combined into a common cable for transmission to subscribers.

Name: Distribution Hub
Description: Location serving as a central point for distribution of communication services. The hub facility will distribute most types of communications services (e.g., voice, data, video, telemetry) to the outlying buildings served from the hub location.

Name: Fiber Node
Description: Optical fiber to a junction box (node). Fiber is terminated in a street cabinet up to several kilometers away from the customer premises, with the final connection being copper.

Name: Wireless Communication
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in operating, maintaining or providing access to facilities for the transmission of voice, data, text, sound, and video using wireless communications networks. Transmission facilities may be based on a single technology or a combination of technologies. Note: Does not include satellite communications systems, which are described in sub-sector 8.3.

Name: Cellular Telephone Facility
Description: Establishments engaged in operating cellular communications. These facilities generally provide two-way communication capability.

Name: Cell Phone Tower
Description: Towers, receivers, and transmitters that handle cell phone traffic within a relatively small area or cell. Also referred to as cell base stations.

Name: Cell Phone Switching Center
Description: A facility that controls the operation of all the cell phone towers in an area and connects the wireless telecommunication system to the wired telecommunication network. Also referred to as mobile telephone switching offices.

Name: Cellular Phone Network Operations Center
Description: Central facilities from which large portions of a cell phone network, perhaps nationwide, are monitored and managed. In general, each wireless communications company has its own network operations center. Larger companies may have more than one center, including backup facilities.

Name: Mobile Switching Office
Description: Primary service delivery node for Global System for Mobile Communication / Code Division Multiple Access (GSM/CDMA), responsible for routing voice calls and Short Message Service (SMS) as well as other services (such as conference calls, FAX and circuit switched data).

Name: Pager Facility
Description: Establishments engaged in operating pager communications. These facilities generally provide one-way text message communication capability although some provide two-way message capability. Note: Some cellular telephone companies provide paging services.

Name: Pager Tower
Description: Towers, receivers, and transmitters that handle pager traffic.

Name: Pager Operations Center
Description: Facilities that accept messages to be sent and direct them to pager towers for transmittal.

Name: Wireless Telecommunication Service Provider Facility
Description: Administrative facilities of companies that own and operate wireless communications networks.

Name: Operations Management
Description: Facilities and assets where operational activities and procedures take place that address critical infrastructure  coordination efforts of operational activities toward improving the physical and cyber security protection of Communications Sector assets. Includes capabilities to ease the flow of information within the sector, across sectors, and with designated federal agencies; also includes addressing issues related to response and recovery under all hazards to assure the continued operation of vital communications services.

Name: Network Operations Center
Description: Central facilities from which large portions of the telephone network are monitored and managed. In general, each communications company has its own network operations center. Larger companies may have more than one center, including backup facilities.

Name: Call Center / Customer Service Center
Description: Central locations where telephone agents, or operators, make outgoing calls or field inbound calls for a company or organization.

Name: Billing Center
Description: Central location that collects invoice information from clients and generates bills on their behalf.

Name: Emergency Operations Center
Description: Central command and control facilities responsible for carrying out the principles of emergency preparedness and emergency management, or disaster management functions at a strategic level in an emergency situation, and ensuring the continuity of operation of a organization or company.

Name: Data Center
Description: Facilities that house the computer systems and related equipment, including the data library. Data entry and systems programming may also come under its jurisdiction. A control section is usually provided that accepts work from and releases output to user departments.

Name: Satellite Communication
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing point-to-point communications services to other establishments in the communications and broadcasting industries by forwarding and receiving communications signals via a system of satellites or reselling satellite communications. Communications conveyed by satellite include voice, data, video, television, radio, text, sound, and other material.

Name: Satellite Communications Ground Station
Description: Facilities that transmit and receive signals from satellites and process communication traffic.  These stations can also provide the connections to other communication networks (e.g., telephone, broadcast television).

Name: Operations Management
Description: Central facilities from which multiple ground stations are monitored and managed.

Name: Network Operations Center
Description: Locations that monitor the operation of a network and usually provide efforts to solve connectivity and network problems. The network operations center (NOC) provides management of the terrestrial infrastructure by looking at configuration management and lock-down status/network systems monitoring. Network systems monitoring control sit in the NOC on the terrestrial side and monitor traffic to and from the terrestrial NOC.

Name: Satellite Operations Center
Description: One or more facilities that supports space segment operations by providing pre-launch planning, launch and early orbit support, and satellite control functions. Satellite operation center (SOC) personnel perform satellite command and control during launch, on-orbit test, and deployment, and assist in major anomaly resolution.

Name: Call Center
Description: Facilities that operate 24/7 to ensure available and reliable terrestrial network services to customers and enterprises.

Name: Billing Center
Description: Central locations that collect invoice information from their clients and generate bills on their behalf.

Name: Emergency Operations Center
Description: Centralized locations where satellite operators implement protocols to aide in terrestrial critical infrastructure restoration efforts in the event of an emergency or crisis operation.

Name: Data Center
Description: Satellite information repositories where precise orbit data and maneuver plans from the operator’s fleets are stored.

Name: Satellite Control Station
Description: Facilities that provide monitoring and control of satellite orbits, orientation, performance, and related parameters. May be co-located with network operations centers.

Name: Satellite Telecommunication Ground Link
Description: Ground communication links in the satellite communications network that connect ground stations, network operations centers, satellite control stations and administration offices. These links also connect ground stations to end user facilities. Includes copper wire, fiber optic, microwave and other technologies.

Name: Communication Satellite
Description: The network of low earth orbit and geostationary orbit communication satellites.

Name: Satellite Telecommunication Service Provider Facility
Description: Administrative facilities of companies that own and operate satellite communications networks.

Name: User Terminal
Description: Satellite user terminals establish command and control streams with various satellite constellations.

Name: Broadband Global Area Network
Description: Global satellite internet network with telephony using portable terminals. The terminals are normally used to connect a laptop computer to broadband Internet in remote locations; however, as long as line-of-sight to the satellite exists, the terminal can be used anywhere. The value of broadband global area network (BGAN) terminals is that unlike other satellite Internet services which require bulky and heavy satellite dishes to connect, a BGAN terminal is about the size of a laptop and can be carried easily. The network is provided by Inmarsat and uses three geostationary satellites called I-4 to provide almost global coverage.

Name: Very Small Aperture Terminal
Description: Satellite communications system that serves home and business users. A Very Small Aperture Terminal (VSAT) end user needs a box that interfaces between the user's computer and an outside antenna with a transceiver. The transceiver receives or sends a signal to a satellite transponder in the sky. The satellite sends and receives signals from an earth station computer that acts as a hub for the system. Each end user is interconnected with the hub station via the satellite in a star topology. For one end user to communicate with another, each transmission has to first go to the hub station which retransmits it via the satellite to the other end user's VSAT. VSAT handles data, voice, and video signals.

Name: Satellite Phone
Description: Type of mobile phone that connects to orbiting satellites instead of terrestrial cell sites.

Name: Internet
Description: Facilities and establishments engaged in the operation of the Internet.

Name: Internet Service Provider
Description: Establishments that provide clients with access to the Internet and generally provide related services such as web hosting, web page designing, and hardware or software consulting related to Internet connectivity.

Name: Point of Presence
Description: Locations where internet service providers (ISPs) have equipment to connect their customers to the rest of the Internet. Such equipment includes routers, digital/analog aggregators, servers, and switches. Larger ISPs have multiple points of presence (POPs). The POP may be located at a facility owned by a communications company. More than one ISP may have POP equipment at the same location.

Name: Network Access Point
Description: Points of access to the Internet used by internet service providers (ISPs) and providers of Internet regional and local subnets. Network access points (NAPs) provide a junction where ISPs exchange traffic and routes. NAPs provide a means of direct connection to the Internet, rather than an intermediate point of exchanging commercial traffic.

Name: Peering Point
Description: Points where Internet Service Providers (ISPs), usually the larger ones, exchange traffic under peering arrangements. Frequently, peering points are located at network access points.

Name: Domain Name Server
Description: The set of computers and databases that translate Internet domain names, which are alphabetic, into numerical Internet Protocol (IP) addresses. Used to locate each computer on the Internet.

Name: Internet Backbone Link
Description: High speed, high capacity communication lines (primarily fiber optic but also other technologies) connecting major metropolitan areas.

Name: Web Search Portal
Description: Establishments operating web sites that use a search engine to generate and maintain extensive databases of Internet addresses and content in an easily searchable format.

Name: Information Services
Description: Facilities engaged in producing and distributing information and cultural products, providing the means to transmit or distribute these products as well as data or communications, and processing data.

Name: Broadcasting
Description: Facilities that create content or acquire the right to distribute content and subsequently broadcast the content.

Name: Radio Broadcasting
Description: Facilities operating radio broadcasting studios and facilities for the transmission of aural programming by radio to the public, to affiliates, or to subscribers.

Name: Radio Network Headquarters
Description: Central locations for radio network operations and control.

Name: Television Broadcasting
Description: Establishments operating television broadcasting studios and facilities for the programming and transmission of programs to the public.

Name: Network Headquarters
Description: Center of operations and control for a television network enterprise.

Name: Local Broadcast Station
Description: Local stations equipped to broadcast radio or television programs.

Name: National News or Sports Transponder
Description: Devices that relay national news or sports via electrical signals not necessarily in the same form or on the same frequency as received.

Name: News Vehicle
Description: Medium through which news is transmitted, expressed, or accomplished (radio broadcast, television, etc.).

Name: Cable and Other Subscription Programming
Description: Facilities and assets associated with television cable and other subscription programming.

Name: Network Headquarters
Description: Central offices of a network.

Name: Local Broadcast Station
Description: Local facilities that create content or acquire the right to distribute content and subsequently broadcast the content.

Name: National News or Sports Transponder
Description: Assets that receive, amplify, and retransmit a signal on a different frequency (broadcast translator).

Name: News Vehicle
Description: Medium through which news is transmitted, expressed, or accomplished (radio broadcast, television, etc.).

Name: Print Media
Description: Facilities engaged in publication of printed materials.

Name: Next Generation Network
Description: High-speed converged circuit-switched and packet-switched networks capable of transporting and routing a multitude of services, including voice, data, video, and multimedia, across variant platforms.

Name: Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Organizations that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight and support to the various components of the communications system.

Name: Federal Communications Agency
Description: Facilities associated with federal agencies dealing with communications including Federal Communications Commission, etc.

Name: State, Local, or Regional Communications Agency
Description: Facilities associated with state, local, and regional agencies that deal with communications in their jurisdictions.

Name: Communications Industry Organization
Description: Facilities associated with industry organizations that provide industry-wide support.

Name: International Communications Organization
Description: Facilities associated with international organizations dealing with communications issues.

Name: Telecomm Hotel
Description: Conditioned floor space owned and operated by a commercial landlord for the purpose of hosting multiple service providers. Tenants may include the incumbent local exchange carrier (ILEC), competitive local exchange carriers (CLEC), Internet service providers (ISP), competitive access providers (CAP), Web hosting operations, or any other non-telecommunications commercial enterprises in need of floor space to support their electronic equipment.

Name: Wired
Description: Telecom hotel equipped with a system of wires, as for electric, telephone, cable television, or computer network connections.

Name: Wireless
Description: Telecom hotel equipped with wireless communication for the transfer of information over a distance without the use of electrical conductors or "wires". Types of wireless communications include cellular telephone, paging, personal communication services, high frequency radio, and other commercial radio services.

Name: Other Communication Facility
Description: Communication facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Postal and Shipping
Description: Assets related to the delivery of letters and small parcels.

Name: United States Postal Service
Description: Assets related to the United States Postal Service. Includes facilities operated by contractors on behalf of the United States Postal Service.

Name: United States Postal Service Administrative Office
Description: United States Postal Service headquarters, district offices, area offices, and engineering offices.

Name: United States Postal Service Information System Center
Description: Data centers that house information systems or are the locations from which information systems are deployed. Includes information system service centers, field computing service centers, and national network service centers. Information systems refer to databases and computing resources.

Name: United States Postal Service Mail Center
Description: Centralized facilities used for sorting and processing mail.

Name: United States Postal Service International Exchange Office
Description: Facilities that receive, concentrate, transfer, dispatch, and distribute mail received from or destined to international locations.

Name: United States Postal Service Processing and Distribution Center
Description: Mail facilities that process and dispatch part or all of both incoming and outgoing mail for a designated service area.

Name: United States Postal Service Airport Mail Center
Description: Mail facilities at airports that receive, concentrate, transfer, dispatch, and distribute mail transported by air.

Name: United States Postal Service Network Distribution Center
Description: Highly mechanized mail processing plants that distribute standard mail in piece and bulk form. (Formerly known as Bulk Mail Centers.)

Name: Other United States Postal Service Mail Center
Description: Other centralized mail processing facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized ( e.g., transportation hubs).

Name: United States Postal Service Retail Center
Description: Facilities serving a specific local area to receive mail and provide other postal services (e.g., post office, retail store fronts).

Name: United States Postal Service Delivery Unit
Description: Facilities serving a specific local area to deliver mail (e.g., post office, other delivery units).

Name: United States Postal Service Vehicle Maintenance Facility
Description: Centralized facilities that provide vehicle maintenance support to a specific local area.

Name: Courier
Description: Commercial companies providing delivery of letters and parcels ( e.g., United Parcel Service, Federal Express, DHL, Airborne).

Name: Courier Administrative Office
Description: Courier company headquarters and regional offices.

Name: Courier Information System Center
Description: Data centers that house information systems or are the locations from which information systems are deployed. Information systems refer to databases and other computing resources.

Name: Courier Processing Center
Description: Centralized facilities for sorting and processing letters and parcels.  Some vehicle maintenance might also be performed at these centers.

Name: Courier International/National Hub
Description: Facilities at airports that receive, concentrate, transfer, dispatch, and distribute letters and parcels to and from international locations and have reach to all hubs and gateways.

Name: Courier Regional Hub
Description: Facilities that receive, concentrate, transfer, dispatch, and distribute letters and parcels primarily within a region, but may also have reach to other regional hubs.  These can be at air facilities, trucking hubs, or a combination of both.

Name: Courier Air Gateway
Description: Facilities at airports that receive, concentrate, transfer, dispatch, and distribute letters and parcels via air, truck, or rail for transport between regional hubs and pickup/delivery stations.

Name: Courier Pickup/Delivery Station
Description: Local facilities that receive, concentrate, transfer, dispatch, and distribute letters and parcels to/from customers.

Name: Courier Retail Facility
Description: Facilities serving a specific local area to collect letters and parcels and provide other customer services.

Name: Courier Aircraft Maintenance Facility
Description: Centralized facilities that provide aircraft and/or vehicle maintenance support.

Name: Other Postal and Shipping Facility
Description: Other postal and shipping facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Healthcare and Public Health
Description: Assets related to the provision of health-related services to individuals (generally referred to as healthcare), the provision of health-related services to populations, including the general community, at the workplace, at academic institutions, and in the military (generally referred to as public health), and the provision of health-related supporting services. Includes public and private facilities.

Name: Direct Patient Healthcare
Description: Facilities providing healthcare services to individuals including diagnosis and treatment of injuries and diseases.

Name: Hospital
Description: Facilities providing medical, diagnostic, and treatment services that include physician, nursing, and other health services to inpatients and the specialized accommodation services required by inpatients.

Name: General Medical and Surgical Hospital
Description: Hospitals primarily engaged in providing diagnostic and medical treatment (both surgical and non-surgical) to inpatients with a of a wide variety of medical conditions.

Name: Private or Private Not-for-Profit General Medical and Surgical Hospital
Description: Hospitals operated by private organizations, both profit and non-profit.

Name: Federal General Medical and Surgical Hospital
Description: Hospitals operated by government organizations (e.g., veterans hospital).

Name: State, Local, or Tribal General Medical and Surgical Hospital
Description: Hospitals operated by state, local, or tribal government organizations (e.g., community hospital).

Name: Psychiatric or Substance Abuse Hospital
Description: Hospitals primarily engaged in providing diagnostic and medical treatment, and monitoring services for inpatients who suffer from mental illness or substance abuse disorders.

Name: Private or Not-for-Profit Psychiatric or Substance Abuse Hospital
Description: Hospitals operated by private organizations, both profit and non-profit.

Name: Federal Psychiatric or Substance Abuse Hospital
Description: Hospitals operated by government organizations (e.g., veterans hospital).

Name: State, Local, or Tribal Psychiatric or Substance Abuse Hospital
Description: Hospitals operated by state, local, or tribal government organizations (e.g., veterans hospital).

Name: Children's Hospital
Description: Hospitals primarily engaged in providing diagnostic and medical treatment to children.

Name: Specialty Hospital
Description: Hospitals primarily engaged in providing diagnostic and medical treatment to inpatients with a specific type of disease or medical condition (e.g., cancer hospital, orthopedic hospital).

Name: Critical Access Hospital
Description: Hospitals that receive funding and support from Medicare that are located in rural and mountainous regions that do not have access to traditional hospital systems. These critical access hospitals may not even be staffed by a physician 24 hours a day. These hospitals have a distinct set of rules and eligibility requirements and number over 1,200 across the nation.

Name: Emergency Department
Description: Facilities in a hospital that provides initial care and stabilization for both urgent and non-urgent patients who may arrive by private or public vehicle. This facility acts as the forefront receiver for the patients from emergency responders, in addition to medical control and communication. It also provides equipment, supply and pharmaceutical caches, housing units for medical personnel and non-medical support members, and augments the final treatment plan (e.g., transfer, admission, treatment and discharge, operating room).

Name: Ambulatory Healthcare Facility
Description: Facilities providing medical, diagnostic, and treatment services that include physician, nursing, and other health services to outpatients.

Name: Family Planning Center
Description: Establishments with medical staff primarily engaged in providing a range of family planning services on an outpatient basis, such as contraceptive services, genetic and prenatal counseling, voluntary sterilization, and therapeutic and medically indicated termination of pregnancy.

Name: Outpatient Mental Health or Substance Abuse Center
Description: Establishments with medical staff primarily engaged in providing outpatient services related to the diagnosis and treatment of mental health disorders and alcohol and other substance abuse. These establishments generally treat patients who do not require inpatient treatment. They may provide a counseling staff and information regarding a wide range of mental health and substance abuse issues and/or refer patients to more extensive treatment programs, if necessary.

Name: Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) Medical Center
Description: Establishments with physicians and other medical staff primarily engaged in providing a range of outpatient medical services to the health maintenance organization (HMO) subscribers with a focus generally on primary health care. These establishments are owned by the HMO.

Name: Kidney Dialysis Center
Description: Establishments with medical staff primarily engaged in providing outpatient kidney or renal dialysis services.

Name: Freestanding Surgical Care Center
Description: Establishments with physicians and other medical staff primarily engaged in providing surgical services (e.g., arthroscopic, cataract, plastic surgery) on an outpatient basis.

Name: Urgent Care Center
Description: Establishments with physicians and other medical staff primarily engaged in providing treatment of unexpected sickness or injuries that are not life threatening but require immediate attention.

Name: Rehabilitation Facility
Description: Establishments providing physical, occupational, and vocational therapy on an outpatient basis to patients recovering from an illness or injury.

Name: Birthing Center
Description: Establishments offering a home-like setting for childbirth. Usually a woman labors, delivers, and recovers in the same room. It's designed for women with low-risk pregnancies.

Name: Prosthetic Limb Center
Description: Establishments providing fitting and servicing of prosthetic limbs.

Name: Community Healthcare Center
Description: Community-based, patient-directed facilities that serve populations with limited access to health care.

Name: Occupational Health Clinic
Description: Facilities provide health services focused on the needs of employees.  Attached to specific industrial or other facilities.

Name: Extended Care Facility
Description: Facilities providing residential care combined with either nursing, supervisory, or other types of care as required by the residents over an extended period of time. Includes facilities such as nursing homes, mental health and substance abuse homes, assisted living facilities, child group foster homes, disabled group homes, halfway houses, orphanages, and others.

Name: Nursing Care Facility
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in providing inpatient nursing and rehabilitative services. The care is generally provided for an extended period of time to individuals requiring nursing care. These establishments have a permanent core staff of registered or licensed practical nurses who, along with other staff, provide nursing and continuous personal care services.

Name: Residential Care Facility
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing residential care and treatment for patients with mental health and substance abuse illnesses and for the elderly. These establishments provide room, board, supervision, counseling services and assistance in daily living, such as housekeeping services. Medical services may or may not be available at these establishments.

Name: Hospice
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing services for terminally ill patients, generally with a prognosis of six months or less.

Name: Health Practitioner Office or Clinic
Description: Offices and clinics of health practitioners having primarily engaged in the independent practice of general or specialized healthcare. Does not include public health offices.

Name: Physicians Office
Description: Offices of health practitioners having the degree of M.D. (Doctor of Medicine) or D.O. (Doctor of Osteopathy) primarily engaged in the independent practice of general or specialized medicine (e.g., anesthesiology, oncology, ophthalmology, psychiatry) or surgery. Includes offices of mental health specialists engaged in independent practice of psychiatry or psychoanalysis.

Name: Dental Office
Description: Offices of health practitioners having the degree of D.M.D. (Doctor of Dental Medicine), D.D.S. (Doctor of Dental Surgery), or D.D.Sc. (Doctor of Dental Science) primarily engaged in the independent practice of general or specialized dentistry or dental surgery.

Name: Mental Health Practitioner Office
Description: Offices of independent mental health practitioners (except physicians) primarily engaged in the diagnosis and treatment of mental, emotional, and behavioral disorders and/or the diagnosis and treatment of individual or group social dysfunction brought about by such causes as mental illness, alcohol and substance abuse, physical and emotional trauma, or stress.

Name: Home Healthcare
Description: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing skilled nursing services in the home, along with a range of the following: personal care services, homemaker and companion services, physical therapy, medical social services, medications, medical equipment and supplies, counseling, 24-hour home care, occupation and vocational therapy, dietary and nutritional services, speech therapy, audiology and high-tech care, such as intravenous therapy.

Name: Public Health Agency
Description: Agencies and departments engaged in public health services.

Name: Clinical Agency/Department
Description: Clinics that are primarily engaged in providing healthcare related to public health issues (e.g., epidemic control, mass vaccination programs). Level of healthcare (e.g., physician, nurse) varies with availability of staff.

Name: Federal Agency
Description: Federal clinics that are primarily engaged in providing healthcare related to public health issues (e.g., epidemic control, mass vaccination programs). Level of healthcare (e.g., physician, nurse) varies with availability of staff.

Name: Regional Agency
Description: Regional clinics that are primarily engaged in providing healthcare related to public health issues (e.g., epidemic control, mass vaccination programs). Level of healthcare (e.g., physician, nurse) varies with availability of staff.

Name: State Agency
Description: State clinics that are primarily engaged in providing healthcare related to public health issues (e.g., epidemic control, mass vaccination programs). Level of healthcare (e.g., physician, nurse) varies with availability of staff.

Name: Local Agency
Description: Local clinics that are primarily engaged in providing healthcare related to public health issues (e.g., epidemic control, mass vaccination programs). Level of healthcare (e.g., physician, nurse) varies with availability of staff.

Name: Tribal Agency
Description: Tribal clinics that are primarily engaged in providing healthcare related to public health issues (e.g., epidemic control, mass vaccination programs). Level of healthcare (e.g., physician, nurse) varies with availability of staff.

Name: Non-Clinical Agency/Department
Description: Facilities that are primarily engaged in providing non-clinical services related to public health.

Name: Federal Agency
Description: Federal facilities that are primarily engaged in providing non-clinical services related to public health.

Name: Regional Agency
Description: Regional facilities that are primarily engaged in providing non-clinical services related to public health.

Name: State Agency
Description: State facilities that are primarily engaged in providing non-clinical services related to public health.

Name: Local Agency
Description: Local facilities that are primarily engaged in providing non-clinical services related to public health.

Name: Tribal Agency
Description: Tribal facilities that are primarily engaged in providing non-clinical services related to public health.

Name: Healthcare Educational Facility
Description: Facilities providing education and training for healthcare professionals.

Name: Medical School
Description: Establishments providing education of physicians.

Name: Dental School
Description: Establishments providing education of dentists.

Name: Nursing School
Description: Establishments providing education of nurses.

Name: Pharmacy School
Description: Establishments providing education of pharmacists.

Name: Allied Health School
Description: Establishments providing education of allied health workers (e.g., medical technologists, therapists, physician assistants).

Name: Health Supporting Facility
Description: Facilities providing supporting functions to healthcare activities.

Name: Medical and Diagnostic Laboratory
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in providing analytic or diagnostic services, including body fluid analysis and diagnostic imaging, generally to the medical profession or to the patient on referral from a health practitioner.

Name: In-Hospital/Clinic Laboratory
Description: Laboratories located in a hospital or clinic.

Name: Stand-Alone Medical and Diagnostic Laboratory
Description: Laboratories not in a hospital or clinic.

Name: Agency-Operated Laboratory
Description: Agency-operated laboratories not in a hospital or clinic.

Name: BSL - Government
Description: BSL government-operated laboratories not in a hospital or clinic.

Name: Private
Description: Privately operated laboratories not in a hospital or clinic.

Name: Public Health Laboratory
Description: Laboratories that focus on providing analytic or diagnostic services that are focused on public health issues (e.g., epidemiologic testing).

Name: Agency-Operated Laboratory
Description: Agency-operated laboratories that focus on providing analytic or diagnostic services that are focused on public health issues (e.g., epidemiologic testing).

Name: BSL - Government
Description: BSL government-operated laboratories that focus on providing analytic or diagnostic services that are focused on public health issues (e.g., epidemiologic testing).

Name: Private
Description: Privately operated laboratories that focus on providing analytic or diagnostic services that are focused on public health issues (e.g., epidemiologic testing).

Name: Other Medical and Diagnostic Laboratory
Description: Medical and diagnostic laboratory facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Agency-Operated
Description: Agency-operated medical and diagnostic laboratory facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: BSL- Government
Description: BSL government-operated medical and diagnostic laboratory facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Privately Operated
Description: Privately operated medical and diagnostic laboratory facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Blood, Organ, or Tissue Facility
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in collecting, storing, and distributing blood and blood products and storing and distributing body organs.

Name: Blood/Blood Component Bank
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in collecting, storing, and distributing blood and blood products.

Name: Organ, Tissue, or Bone Marrow Center
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in storing and distributing body organs.

Name: Pharmaceuticals/Biopharmaceutical Production
Description: Assets involved with medicinal chemical and biologic products.

Name: Pharmaceutical/Biopharmaceutical Manufacturing Facility
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in manufacturing biological and medicinal products, processing (e.g., grading, grinding, milling) botanical drugs and herbs, isolating active medicinal principals from botanical drugs and herbs, and/or manufacturing pharmaceutical products intended for internal and external consumption in such forms as ampoules, tablets, capsules, vials, ointments, powders, solutions, and suspensions.

Name: Pharmaceutical/Biopharmaceutical Storage or Stockpile
Description: Facilities for the storage and stockpiling of pharmaceuticals.

Name: Strategic National Stockpile
Description: Facilities that house stockpiles of pharmaceuticals (and other medical supplies and equipment) maintained by the federal government (Centers for Disease Control and Prevention) for use in large scale emergencies.  Includes receipt, staging, and storage (RSS) warehouses and RSS warehouse distribution sites.

Name: Military Stockpile
Description: Facilities that house stockpiles of pharmaceuticals (and other medical supplies and equipment) maintained by the military for use in military operations.

Name: Federal Agency Stockpile
Description: Facilities that house stockpiles of pharmaceuticals (and other medical supplies and equipment) maintained by a federal agency for use in federal agency operations.

Name: State or Local Stockpile
Description: Facilities that house stockpiles of pharmaceuticals (and other medical supplies and equipment) maintained by state or municipal agencies for use in large scale emergencies.

Name: Hospital Stockpile
Description: Facilities that house stockpiles of pharmaceuticals (and other medical supplies and equipment) maintained by hospitals.

Name: Commercial Pharmaceutical Storage for Wholesale Distribution
Description: Facilities used for the storage of pharmaceuticals used for wholesale distribution to hospitals, pharmacies, and other distributors.

Name: Pharmaceutical/Biopharmaceutical Distribution Center
Description: Facilities engaged in retailing prescription or non-prescription drugs and medicines and retail medical supplies and equipment. Includes pharmacies, drug stores, and apothecaries.

Name: Mail-Order Pharmacy
Description: Facilities that dispense a prescription drug or device under a prescription drug order and deliver the drug or device to a patient, by the United States mail, a common carrier, or a delivery service. Mail-order pharmacies include, but are not limited to, pharmacies that do business via the Internet or other electronic media.

Name: Pharmacy
Description: Facilities that dispense a prescription drug or device under a prescription drug order.

Name: Tribal Pharmacy
Description: Tribal facilities that dispense a prescription drug or device under a prescription drug order.

Name: Medical Supplies, Devices, or Equipment
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in providing medical supplies, devices, and equipment.

Name: Medical Supplies, Devices, or Equipment Manufacturing Facility
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in manufacturing medical supplies and equipment. Examples of products made by these establishments are laboratory apparatus and furniture, surgical and medical instruments, surgical appliances and supplies, dental equipment and supplies, orthodontic goods, dentures, and orthodontic appliances.
CrosssectorReference:
4.8.4 Subsistence and Medical Equipment Manufacture,
18.17.1 Medical Equipment and Supplies Manufacturing

Name: Medical Supplies, Devices, or Equipment Storage or Stockpile
Description: Facilities engaged in the distribution of medical equipment and supplies to hospitals, pharmacies, healthcare practitioners, and other users.

Name: Strategic National Stockpile
Description: Facilities associated with the United States' national repository of emergency medical countermeasures, including those for radiation events.

Name: Military Stockpile
Description: Facilities associated with a military repository of emergency medical countermeasures.

Name: Federal Stockpile
Description: Facilities associated with a federal repository of emergency medical countermeasures.

Name: State or Local Stockpile
Description: Facilities associated with a state or local repository of emergency medical countermeasures.

Name: Hospital Stockpile
Description: Facilities associated with a hospital repository of emergency medical countermeasures.

Name: Commercial Medical Supplies, Devices and Equipment Storage for Wholesale Distributors
Description: Facilities engaged in the distribution of commercial medical equipment and supplies to hospitals, pharmacies, healthcare practitioners, and other users.

Name: Medical Practitioner Registry or Referral Center
Description: Facilities that provide directories of medical personnel.
