Name: Physician Referral Center
Description: Establishments that maintain and disseminate information on physicians, dentists, and other medical professionals.

Name: Nursing Registry
Description: Facilities associated with agencies that provide the services of registered or licensed practical nurses for short-term, temporary assignments.

Name: Healthcare, Public Health, or Vital Records Information Network
Description: Facilities that store and disseminate healthcare, public health, and vital records information.  Includes facilities to communicate health alert messages to federal, state, and local agencies.

Name: Vital Records Center
Description: Establishments that maintain and disseminate vital records (e.g., births, deaths).

Name: Public Health Data Center
Description: Establishments that maintain and disseminate public health data and statistics (e.g., epidemiology centers, disease breakout monitoring).

Name: Poison Control Center
Description: Establishments that maintain and disseminate information on poisonous substances.

Name: Other Healthcare Information Network
Description: Facilities and assets associated with healthcare, public health, or vital records information network not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Medical Research Facility
Description: Facilities engaged in medical research and experimental development. Includes research on drugs, treatments, medical equipment, epidemiology, and related activities.

Name: Industrial Medical Research Facility
Description: Medical research facilities owned and operated by a private sector organization, either profit or non-profit.  Includes facilities conducting clinical, pharmaceutical, biological, and other medical-related research.

Name: Academic Medical Research Facility
Description: Medical research facilities owned and operated by an educational institution (e.g., college, university).  Includes facilities conducting clinical, pharmaceutical, biological, and other medical-related research.

Name: Government Medical Research Facility
Description: Medical research facilities owned and operated by a government agency. Includes facilities conducting clinical, pharmaceutical, biological, and other medical-related research.

Name: Health Insurance Facility
Description: Facilities associated with organizations providing health insurance.  Includes government and private insurance organizations.

Name: Government Health Insurance Agency (Includes Tribal Agencies)
Description: Facilities associated with government agencies providing health insurance.  Includes federal Medicare, Medicaid, and tribal, state, and local agencies.

Name: Private Health Insurance Company
Description: Facilities associated with private companies providing health insurance.

Name: Fatality/Mortuary Facility
Description: Facilities for dealing with end-of-life issues.

Name: Morgue
Description: Coroner facilities dealing with the investigation and determination of cause of death.  Coroners are elected officials and may or may not have medical training.

Name: Funeral Home or Crematorium
Description: Medical examiner facilities dealing with the investigation and determination of cause of death.  Medical examiners are physicians, typically a forensic pathologist who is specifically trained in death investigation.

Name: Cemetery
Description: Facilities primarily engaged in operating sites or structures reserved for the internment of human remains.

Name: Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Organizations that provide professional, technical, operation, and business oversight and support to the various components of the healthcare system.

Name: Federal Healthcare Agency or Department
Description: Federal agencies dealing with healthcare such as Department of Health and Human Services, Food and Drug Administration, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.  Does not include clinical facilities involved in direct patient care; these are categorized elsewhere.

Name: State Healthcare and Public Health Agency or Department
Description: State agencies dealing with healthcare and public health in their jurisdictions. Does not include clinical facilities involved in direct patient care; these are categorized elsewhere.

Name: Professional Licensing Board
Description: Entities associated with licensing professionals.

Name: Local Healthcare and Public Health Agency or Department (Includes Tribal Agencies)
Description: Tribal, municipal, and other local agencies dealing with healthcare and public health in their jurisdictions. Does not include clinical facilities involved in direct patient care; these are categorized elsewhere.

Name: Regional Healthcare and Public Health Agency or Department
Description: Organizations that coordinate healthcare and public health activities across jurisdictions.

Name: Occupational Health or Environmental Health Program
Description: Healthcare industry organizations that provide industry-wide support.

Name: Healthcare Industry Organization
Description: Professional societies (e.g., American Medical Association, American Dental Association) and industry trade organizations.

Name: Accreditation Organization
Description: International organizations dealing with healthcare and public health issues.

Name: Other Healthcare and Public Health Entity
Description: Other healthcare and public health facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Transportation
Description: The Transportation sector is comprised of a multitude of networks of transportation systems. Systems vary in size and complexity, but all modes of transportation have one element in common - they have defined origin and destination points, and the assets that comprise the system of interest exist for the sole purpose of facilitating the flow of either people or products. For the purposes of this taxonomy, assets are comprised of nodes and linkages. One example of a node is a rail yard; one example of a link is the portion of rail track that joins two rail yards. Hence, users of the transportation portion of this taxonomy must first think in terms of specifically defined systems and the flow of either people or products through the defined systems. The individual concepts provided in this taxonomy, are the physical elements that comprise the systems.

Name: Aviation
Description: Assets involved in the aviation industry.

Name: Aviation Conveyance
Description: All types of aircraft.

Name: Airport
Description: Fields for handling aircraft landings and takeoffs.

Name: Certificated Airport
Description: Airports that hold certificates under Federal regulations (14 Code of Federal Regulations [CFR] Part 139). Includes runways, taxiways, apron areas, passenger terminals, baggage handling areas, cargo terminals, maintenance facilities, parking lots and garages, customs and immigration facilities (if handling international flights), and other ancillary service facilities. Using the applicability paragraph of Part 139, a certificated airport is defined as "Any airport in any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, or any territory or possession of the United States serving any (1) Scheduled passenger-carrying operations of an air carrier operating aircraft designed for more than 9 passenger seats, as determined by the aircraft type certificate issued by a competent civil aviation authority and (2) Unscheduled passenger-carrying operations of an air carrier operating aircraft designed for at least 31 passenger seats, as determined by the aircraft type certificate issued by a competent civil aviation authority. Included are those portions of a joint-use or shared-use airport that are within the authority of a person serving passenger-carrying operations. This does not include (1) Airports serving scheduled air carrier operations only by reason of being designated as an alternate airport (2) Airports operated by the United States (3) Airports located in the State of Alaska that only serve scheduled operations of small air carrier aircraft and do not serve scheduled or unscheduled operations of large air carrier aircraft (4) Airports located in the State of Alaska during periods of time when not serving operations of large air carrier aircraft or (5) Heliports.            "

Name: Category X Airport
Description: An airport where screening is performed pursuant to 49 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Part 1544.101(a)(1) or 1546.101(a) and the number of annual enplanements is five million or more and international enplanements of one million or more.

Name: Category I Airport
Description: An airport where screening is performed pursuant to 49 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Part 1544.101(a)(1) or 1546.101(a) and the number of annual enplanements is 1.25 million or more.

Name: Category II Airport
Description: An airport where screening is performed pursuant to 49 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Part 1544.101(a)(1) or 1546.101(a) and the number of annual enplanements is 250,000 or more, but less than 1.25 million.

Name: Category III Airport
Description: An airport where screening is performed pursuant to 49 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Part 1544.101(a)(1) or 1546.101(a) and the number of annual enplanements is less than 250,000.

Name: Category IV Airport
Description: An airport with screening operations pursuant to 49 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Part 1544.101(a)(2), (b) or (f) or 1546.101(b) or (c).

Name: Non-Certificated Airport
Description: Airports that do not hold certificates under Federal regulations (14 Code of Federal Regulations [CFR] Part 139). Includes runways, taxiways, apron areas, and other facilities. Using the applicability paragraph of Part 139, a non-certificated airport is defined as "Any airport with scheduled passenger-carrying operations of an air carrier operating aircraft designed for 9 or less passenger seats or unscheduled passenger-carrying operations of an air carrier operating aircraft designed for 30 or less passenger seats and includes (1) Airports serving scheduled air carrier operations only by reason of being designated as an alternate airport (2) Airports operated by the United States (3) Airports located in the State of Alaska that only serve scheduled operations of small air carrier aircraft and do not serve scheduled or unscheduled operations of large air carrier aircraft (4) Airports located in the State of Alaska during periods of time when not serving operations of large air carrier aircraft or (5) Heliports."

Name: Public Airport
Description: Public use airports that are not certificated (e.g., some general aviation airports).

Name: Private Airport
Description: Airports owned by an individual or a business that cannot be used without the owner's permission. May or may not have any facilities beyond a runway or landing strip.

Name: Military Airfield
Description: Airfields owned and operated by the military. Includes runways, taxiways, apron areas, maintenance and other facilities.

Name: Air Force Airfield
Description: Airfields operated by the Air Force.

Name: Army Airfield
Description: Airfields operated by the Army.

Name: Navy Airfield
Description: Airfields operated by the Navy.

Name: Marine Corps Airfield
Description: Airfields operated by the Marine Corps.

Name: Coast Guard Airfield
Description: Airfields operated by the Coast Guard.

Name: Foreign Airport
Description: Airports outside the U.S.

Name: Air Traffic Control or Navigation Facility
Description: Control centers, radar installations, and communication facilities.

Name: Air Route Traffic Control Facility
Description: Facilities that provide air traffic service to aircraft operating within controlled airspace, and principally during the en route phase of flight.

Name: Airport Traffic Control Tower
Description: Facilities providing for the safe, orderly, and expeditious flow of traffic on and in the vicinity of an airport and, when designated, provide for the separation of aircraft in the terminal areas.

Name: Flight Service Station
Description: Facilities that provide information (e.g., weather, route, terrain, flight plans) for private pilots flying into and out of small airports and rural areas. Also assists pilots in emergencies and coordinates search-and-rescue operations for missing or overdue aircraft.

Name: Other Air Traffic Control or Navigation Facility
Description: Air traffic control or navigation facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Military Facility
Description: Facilities for the processing, integration, and assembly of military launch vehicles and payloads, launch and recovery operations, and range support for military launches.

Name: Launch Vehicle
Description: Military launch vehicles.

Name: Launch Facility
Description: Facilities for launching military space vehicles.

Name: Mission Control Facility
Description: Facilities for control of military space vehicles after launch.

Name: Satellite
Description: Military satellites.

Name: Commercial Facility
Description: Spaceports and facilities for the processing, integration, and assembly of civilian and commercial orbital and suborbital launch vehicles and payloads, launch and recovery operations, and range support for civilian and commercial space activities.

Name: Launch Vehicle
Description: Civilian and commercial launch vehicles.

Name: Launch Facility
Description: Facilities for launching civilian space vehicles.

Name: Mission Control Facility
Description: Facilities for control of civilian space vehicles after launch.

Name: Satellite
Description: Civilian satellites (e.g., weather, communications, research).

Name: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Facility
Description: Spaceports and facilities for the processing, integration, and assembly of National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) orbital and suborbital launch vehicles and payloads, launch and recovery operations, and range support for NASA space activities.
CrosssectorReference: 15.6.2 National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Facility

Name: Launch Vehicle
Description: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) launch vehicles.

Name: Launch Facility
Description: Facilities for launching National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) space vehicles.

Name: Mission Control Facility
Description: Facilities for control of National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) space vehicles after launch.

Name: Satellite
Description: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) satellites (e.g., weather, communications, research).

Name: Aviation Sector Command Control Communication Coordination Facility
Description: Facilities involved in providing, maintaining, or restoring a safe and secure aviation system. Includes facilities such as Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) Air Traffic Control System Command Center, National Capitol Region Command Center, Transportation Security Operations Center, and North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) Cheyenne Mountain Operations Center.

Name: Other Aviation Facility
Description: Aviation facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Railroad
Description: Assets involved in rail transportation.

Name: Railroad Conveyance
Description: All types of trains.

Name: Freight Conveyance
Description: Trains that handle the movement of goods from producer to consumer.

Name: Passenger Conveyance
Description: Trains that handle the movement of people by rail.

Name: Passenger Train Long Distance/Intercity
Description: Trains that handle the movement of people by rail between cities and towns other than for daily commuting.

Name: Passenger Train Commuter
Description: An electric or diesel propelled railway for urban passengers traveling short distances between a central city and adjacent suburbs. Service must be operated on a regular basis by or under contract with a transit operator for the purpose of transporting passengers within urban areas, or between urban areas and outlying areas. Such rail service, using either locomotive hauled or self propelled railroad passenger cars, is generally characterized by multi-trip tickets, specific station to station fares, railroad employment practices, and usually only one or two stations in the central business district. Intercity rail service is excluded, except for that portion of such service that is operated by or under contract with a public transit agency for predominantly commuter services, which means that for any given trip segment (e.g., distance between any two stations), more than 50% of the average daily ridership travels on the train at least three times a week. Also called metropolitan rail, regional rail, or suburban rail.

Name: Railroad Right of Way
Description: Routes along which trains operate.

Name: Railroad Track
Description: Main line tracks, sidings, switches, and crossovers.

Name: Strategic Rail Corridor Network (STRACNET) Track
Description: Track identified as part of the Strategic Rail Corridor Network (STRACNET) and necessary to national defense activities.

Name: Other Track
Description: Railroad track facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Railroad Bridge
Description: Bridges carrying rail traffic. May also carry commuter rail traffic and/or road traffic.

Name: Operable Bridge
Description: Bridges that are staffed.

Name: Railroad Tunnel
Description: Tunnels carrying rail traffic. May also carry commuter rail traffic and/or road traffic.

Name: Railroad Yard
Description: Areas having a network of tracks and sidings for handling rail cars.

Name: Rail Yard - Local
Description: A railroad yard used primarily for makeup and breakdown of local trains. Provides storage and maintenance of rail cars and/or locomotives. May also contain an area to load and unload cars.

Name: Rail Yard - Classification
Description: A railroad yard with special facilities to efficiently group rail cars according to destination to facilitate the makeup and breakdown of trains. May have areas adjacent for the loading/unloading of cars.

Name: Rail Yard - Intermodal
Description: A railroad yard that is used specifically for handling the transfer of containers and/or trailers between trains and other modes of transport (e.g., truck, ship). Note: Included in this category are facilities that have the label "Inland Port." These facilities, in spite of the label, handle rail-to-road transfers. They are labeled Inland Ports since all traffic moves to and from the facility by rail to the marine docks.

Name: Rail Yard - Hazardous Materials
Description: A railroad yard that has special facilities for handling hazardous materials (HAZMAT).

Name: Railroad Station
Description: Sites along and at the end of rail lines to which service is provided.

Name: Railroad Passenger Station
Description: Sites along or at the end of rail lines for the boarding of passengers on trains for either long distance/intercity trains or commuter trains. May include connections to heavy rail, light rail, mass transit, urban rapid transit, buses, or other modes of transport.

Name: Railroad Operations Center
Description: Facilities to provide operational control of railroads.

Name: Railroad Dispatch or Operations Control Center
Description: Facilities where railroad personnel monitor and control the movement of trains.

Name: Railroad Communications Center
Description: Facilities and equipment where railroad communications are handled.

Name: Railroad Signaling Facility or Equipment
Description: Facilities and equipment used to control signals used to direct train traffic.

Name: Other Staffed Facility
Description: Railroad operations center facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Other Railroad Facility
Description: Railroad facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Road
Description: Assets involved in road transportation.

Name: Roadway or Supporting Facility
Description: Facilities supporting road transport.

Name: Roadway
Description: Highways and roads for motor vehicles. Note: Some roads are designated as part of the Strategic Highway Network (STRAHNET).

Name: Limited Access Highway
Description: Expressways, freeways, and toll roads.

Name: Multi-Lane Non-Limited Access Highway
Description: Roads with more than one lane in each direction and with many access points.

Name: Two Lane Numbered Highway
Description: Numbered routes with one lane in each direction.

Name: Special Purpose Road
Description: Includes evacuation routes, snow emergency routes, hazardous material (HAZMAT) routes, and truck routes.

Name: Other Road
Description: Roadway facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Road Bridge
Description: Bridges carrying road traffic. May also carry rail and/or pedestrian traffic.

Name: Operable Bridge
Description: Draw bridges, lift bridges, swing bridges, and pontoon bridges

Name: Road Tunnel
Description: Tunnels carrying road traffic. May also carry rail and/or pedestrian traffic.

Name: Highway Rest or Service Area
Description: Service facilities attached to highways.

Name: Highway Rest Stop
Description: Parking areas attached to highways without fuel or maintenance facilities. May have food service facilities or tourist information centers.

Name: Highway Service Area
Description: Parking areas attached to highways with fuel and vehicle maintenance facilities. Includes services provided to automobiles and/or trucks. May have food service facilities.

Name: Vehicle Weigh Station
Description: Scales used to ensure vehicles (primarily trucks) are within weight limits.

Name: Truck Stop
CrosssectorReference:
1.4.1.1.2 Truck Stop
1.4.1.2.2 U.S. Border Truck Stop

Name: Road Transportation Support Facility
Description: Facilities providing supporting services to road transportation.

Name: Operations or Traffic Management Center
Description: Centers, usually in urban areas, that monitor and control the flow of road traffic though remote operation of traffic signals and barriers.

Name: Road International Border Facility
Description: Facilities providing immigration and customs control at borders with Canada and Mexico.

Name: Motor Vehicle Fueling Station
Description: Facilities storing and dispensing motor vehicle fuels (e.g., gasoline, diesel, gasohol) to consumers. May include maintenance and repair facilities.

Name: Signalization or Related Facility
Description: Traffic cameras, dynamic message signs, and other sensing devices.

Name: Trucking
Description: Vehicles and facilities related to freight movement by truck.

Name: Truck Conveyance
Description: All types of trucks.

Name: Truck Terminal
Description: Facilities operated by a trucking company handling a large number of truck arrivals and departures. Used for handling and temporary storage of freight pending transfer to other locations. In general, freight is stored at a terminal for relatively short periods (e.g., hours, days). Less-than-truckload (LTL) terminals have buildings where smaller quantities of freight are broken apart and reassembled based on destination. Truckload (TL) facilities handle only full truckloads and typically have large open spaces for truck parking and possibly small or no buildings. Both LTL and TL terminals generally have truck maintenance facilities.

Name: Truck Terminal Hazardous Materials Facility
Description: Facilities at truck terminals specially designed to handle hazardous materials (HAZMAT).

Name: Truck Terminal Non-Hazardous Materials Facility
Description: Facilities at truck terminals designed to handle materials other than hazardous materials.

Name: Truck Rental Facility
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in renting or leasing, without drivers, trucks, truck tractors, or semitrailers.

Name: Truck Dispatch Center
Description: Facilities where communication equipment is located and trucks are dispatched.

Name: Truck Operations Center
Description: Facilities where fleet operations are coordinated.

Name: Over-the-Road Motorcoach System
Description: Bus system providing service principally outside a single metropolitan area and its adjacent non-urban areas. Includes both regularly scheduled and charter bus service. Does not include urban mass transit bus systems or school bus service, which are categorized under mass transit.

Name: Motorcoach Conveyance
Description: All types of buses.

Name: Over-the-Road Motorcoach Passenger Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to board and unload passengers and luggage. May be a dedicated facility (e.g., in an urban area) or may be a drop-off point (e.g., in a rural area). May have multi-modal facilities (e.g., rail, mass transit).

Name: Over-the-Road Motorcoach Facility
Description: Parking and maintenance facilities for buses.

Name: Storage Facility
Description: Facilities utilized for parking over-the-road motorcoaches.

Name: Maintenance Facility
Description: Facilities where routine and specific maintenance is performed on over-the-road motorcoaches.

Name: Over-the-Road Motorcoach Operations Center
Description: Facilities where fleet operations are coordinated.

Name: Over-the-Road Motorcoach Dispatch Center
Description: Facilities where communication equipment is located and buses are dispatched.

Name: School Bus System
Description: Bus transportation systems for transport of children to and from school and school-related events.

Name: School Bus Conveyance
Description: All types of school buses.

Name: School Bus Route
Description: Routes followed by school buses. Usually streets shared with other vehicles and pedestrians.

Name: School Bus Stop
Description: Stops for loading and unloading children. May be in a terminal with connections to other transport modes.

Name: School Bus Maintenance Facility
Description: Storage and maintenance facilities for school buses.

Name: School Bus Dispatch Center
Description: Facilities where school bus personnel monitor and control the movement of buses.

Name: School Bus Communication Center
Description: Facilities where communication equipment is located and school bus fleet operations are coordinated.

Name: Other Road Facility
Description: Road facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Maritime
Description: Assets involved in the movement of passengers and freight by water.

Name: Vessel
Description: Marine vessels.

Name: Shallow Draft Vessel
Description: Vessels with less than 15 ft draft.

Name: Tug or Towboat
Description: Harbor tugs, line-haul towboats, and offshore tugs.

Name: Small Vehicle/Passenger Ferry
Description: Vessels designed to carry relatively small vehicles (e.g., passenger cars, small vans) and passengers. Includes commuter ferries. Generally certificated to carry <150 passengers.

Name: River Ferry
Description: Vessels designed to carry small vehicles and a small number of passengers; operates primarily on inland waterways.

Name: Excursion/Tour Boat
Description: Vessels designed to carry a small number of passengers. Includes dinner cruise boats, tour boats, and sightseeing boats.

Name: Supply/Work Boat
Description: Offshore supply, inshore workboat, police/fire boat.

Name: Recreational Vessel
Description: Privately owned pleasure craft <150 length overall.

Name: Barge - Tank
Description: Barges designed to carry liquids in tanks.

Name: Barge - Hopper
Description: Barges designed to carry bulk solids.

Name: Barge - Gas
Description: Barges designed to carry gaseous materials.

Name: Deep Draft Vessel
Description: Vessels with draft of 15 feet or more.

Name: General Cargo Ship
Description: General cargo, between-deck, multipurpose, heavy lift, and other vessels.

Name: Container Ship
Description: Vessels specially designed to transport containerized cargo.

Name: Dry Bulk Cargo Ship
Description: Vessels specially designed to transport dry bulk cargo (e.g., ore, grain).

Name: Tank Ship
Description: Vessels specially designed to transport liquid cargo (e.g., chemicals).

Name: Gas Carrier Ship
Description: Vessels specially designed to transport gaseous material.

Name: Roll-On/Roll-Off or Pure Car Carrier
Description: Vessels specially designed to transport vehicles and to load and unload them under their own power.

Name: Cruise Ship
Description: Vessels specially designed to carry passengers on cruise route, returning to the port of origin.

Name: Large Vehicle/Passenger Ferry
Description: Vessels designed to carry large vehicles (e.g., large trucks) and passengers. Includes commuter ferries. Generally certificated to carry 500 or more passengers.

Name: Medium Vehicle/Passenger Ferry
Description: Vessels designed to carry medium size vehicles (e.g., trucks) and passengers. Generally certificated to carry 150 to 499 passengers.

Name: Military Combatant Vessel
Description: Military warship.

Name: Military Support Vessel
Description: Military vessels other than warships.

Name: Other Deep Draft Vessel
Description: Deep draft vessel facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Port
Description: Facilities designed to dock, load, and unload marine vessels.

Name: Shallow Draft Port
Description: Ports capable of handling vessels with drafts less than 15 feet.

Name: Shallow Draft General Cargo Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to handle a variety of general cargo. These facilities may have transit sheds, open storage areas, and warehouses on site.

Name: Shallow Draft Dry Bulk Cargo Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to load or unload flowable dry commodities. These facilities may have ship loaders or unloaders on the wharf, or may utilize mobile cranes. Storage may be in structures (e.g., silos, sheds) or open (e.g., on pads, in bins).

Name: Shallow Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to load or unload liquid commodities. These facilities have pipelines and manifolds at the waterside. They may or may not have wharves. Those without wharves have cells or "dolphins" for tying up vessels. These terminals may have tank storage on site or may feed pipelines directly from the vessel.

Name: Shallow Draft Barge Fleeting Area
Description: A parking or staging area for barges awaiting loading, unloading, or transport.

Name: Shallow Draft Passenger Terminal
Description: A terminal designed to handle the embarkation and debarkation of passengers from a vessel.

Name: Deep Draft Port
Description: Ports capable of handling vessels with drafts of 15 feet or more.

Name: Deep Draft General Cargo Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to handle a variety of general cargo. These facilities may have transit sheds, open storage areas, and warehouses on site. Includes break bulk, container, heavy-lift, roll-on/roll-off (RO/RO), and parcel-sized dry bulk shipments.

Name: Deep Draft Containerized Cargo Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to move cargo containers to and from vessels. These terminals have rail-mounted gantry cranes on the wharf, a container yard served by rubber-tired gantries (RTGs), straddle carriers, top-lift or side-lift forklifts, and a container freight station for stuffing and unstuffing containers.

Name: Deep Draft Dry Bulk Cargo Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to load or unload flowable dry commodities. These facilities may have ship loaders or unloaders on the wharf, or may utilize mobile cranes. Storage may be in structures (e.g., silos, sheds) or open (e.g., on pads, in bins).

Name: Deep Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal - Crude/Product
Description: Terminals designed to load or unload liquid commodities. These facilities have pipelines and manifolds at the waterside. They may or may not have wharves. Those without wharves have cells or "dolphins" for tying up vessels. These terminals may have tank storage on site or may feed pipelines directly from the vessel.
CrosssectorReference:
5.2.2.2.2 Crude Oil Marine Terminal
5.2.3.2.2 Port Handling Crude Oil
5.2.5.1.2 Petroleum Product Marine Bulk Terminal

Name: Deep Draft Liquid Bulk Cargo Terminal - Chemical
Description: Terminals designed to load or unload liquid chemical commodities. These facilities have pipelines and manifolds at the waterside. They may or may not have wharves. Those without wharves have cells or "dolphins" for tying up vessels. These terminals may have tank storage on site or may feed pipelines directly from the vessel.

Name: Deep Draft Bulk Gas Cargo Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to load or unload gas commodities. These facilities have pipelines and manifolds at the waterside. They may or may not have wharves. Those without wharves have cells or "dolphins" for tying up vessels. These terminals may have tank storage on site or may feed pipelines directly from the vessel.

Name: Deep Draft Industrial Cargo Terminal
Description: Terminals co-located with an industrial facility (e.g., industrial factory, refinery). Generally handle dry or liquid bulk materials that are processed at the industrial facility.

Name: Off-Shore Terminal or Platform
Description: Terminals designed to handle bulk liquid or gas shipments in open seas. Berthing of vessels is normally done at anchoring buoys. Currently used mostly for crude oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) cargo. Does not include oil and gas exploration or production platforms.

Name: Outer Continental Shelf Crude Oil Platform
Description: Sea-based facilities used for the extraction of crude oil. This category includes equipment such as gathering lines used to bring the output on shore. Also referred to as off-shore drilling platforms or off-shore rigs. Note: Natural gas is sometimes extracted from the same wells that are used to extract oil. This is referred to as "associated gas."

Name: Outer Continental Shelf Natural Gas Platform
Description: Sea-based facilities used for the extraction of natural gas. This category includes equipment such as gathering lines used to bring the output on shore. Also referred to as off-shore drilling platforms or off-shore rigs.

Name: Cruise Ship Passenger Terminal
Description: Terminals designed to handle ocean-going cruise ships.

Name: Ferry Terminal
Description: Specially designed docks to allow drive-on/drive-off of vehicles or conventional piers with alongside docking.

Name: Port Public Access Area
Description: Public gathering places in a port, such as parks, fishing piers, dining/shopping sites, etc. May have large numbers of people gathered for events.

Name: Military or Strategic Deep Draft Port
Waterway
Description: Navigable waterways capable of carrying marine traffic.

Name: Inland Waterway
Description: Natural waterways (e.g., rivers, lakes, bayous, estuaries) capable of carrying marine traffic.

Name: Intracoastal Waterway
Description: Partly natural, partly manmade waterways providing sheltered passage for commercial and leisure boats along the U.S. Atlantic coast and along the Gulf of Mexico coast.

Name: Navigation Lock
Description: Walled section of a river or canal, closed by water gates at both ends, in which the water level can be raised or lowered by means of valves or sluiceways to match the level in the upper or lower reach, as desired. When the levels are the same, the water gate is opened to permit a vessel to enter or leave the lock.

Name: Canal
Description: A constructed channel, usually open, that conveys water by gravity to farms, municipalities, etc. Artificial watercourse of perceptible extent, with a definite bed and banks to confine and conduct continuously or periodically flowing water.

Name: Dam
Description: Water retention structures used for irrigation, electricity generation, water supply storage, flood control, navigation, fisheries, recreation, sediment and hazardous materials control, or mine tailings impoundments. Many dams have multiple uses.

Name: Maritime Supporting Facility
Description: Facilities supporting the operation of marine vessels.

Name: Navigation Facility
Description: Facilities providing marine navigation support.

Name: Lighthouse or Beacon
Description: Land-based facilities to mark shorelines or other marine hazards.

Name: Buoy
Description: Water-anchored markers to designate channel boundaries or underwater hazards.

Name: Electronic Navigation Facility
Description: Radar beacon (RACON) and Long-range navigation system (LORAN-C) facilities used to provide navigation support on inland waterways and coastlines.

Name: Emergency Search and Rescue Facility
Description: Facilities equipped to respond to maritime emergencies.

Name: U.S. Coast Guard Marine Emergency Response Facility
Description: Coast Guard stations (marine, air) providing emergency search and rescue vessels, aircraft, equipment, communications, and personnel.

Name: State or Local Marine Emergency Response Facility
Description: State and local stations providing emergency search and rescue capabilities. May be attached to the local fire department.

Name: Other Maritime Facility
Description: Maritime facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Mass Transit
Description: Facilities associated with a conveyance that provides regular and continuing general or special transportation to the public, but does not include school bus, charter, or sightseeing transportation.

Name: Rail Mass Transit
Description: Facilities associated with systems for carrying transit passengers described by specific right-of-way, technology and operational features.

Name: Heavy Rail Transit
Description: Electric railways, to include metro, subway, rapid transit or rapid rail, with the capacity for a heavy volume of traffic. It is characterized by high speed and rapid acceleration passenger rail cars operating singly or in multi-car trains on fixed rails, separate rights-of-way from which all other vehicular and foot traffic are excluded, sophisticated signaling, and high platform loading.

Name: Light Rail Transit
Description: Lightweight passenger rail cars, to include streetcars, tramways, or trolleys, operating singly (or in short, usually two-car, trains) on fixed rails in right-of-way that is not separated from other traffic for much of the way. Light rail vehicles are typically driven electrically with power being drawn from an overhead electric line via a trolley or a pantograph.

Name: Commuter Rail
Description: Electric or diesel propelled railway, to include metropolitan rail, regional rail, or suburban rail, for urban passenger train service consisting of local short distance travel operating between a central city and adjacent suburbs. Intercity rail service is excluded, except for that portion of such service that is operated by or under contract with a public transit agency for predominantly commuter services.

Name: Other Rail Transit
Description: Rail transit facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Rail Transit Passenger Station
Description: A station on a rail transit line that provides passenger loading and unloading. May be above or below ground. May connect with other modes of transport.

Name: Rail Transit Right of Way
Description: Rail transit track, bridges, and tunnels.

Name: Rail Transit Track
Description: Main line tracks, sidings, switches, and crossovers.

Name: Rail Transit Bridge
Description: Bridges carrying rail transit traffic. May also carry freight and intercity passenger rail traffic and/or road traffic.

Name: Rail Transit Tunnel
Description: Tunnels carrying rail transit traffic. May also carry freight and intercity passenger rail traffic and/or road traffic.

Name: Rail Transit Yard
Description: Areas having a network of tracks and sidings used primarily for makeup, breakdown, storage, and maintenance of trains.

Name: Rail Transit Dispatch or Operations Control Center
Description: Facilities where rail transit personnel monitor and control the movement of trains.

Name: Rail Transit Communications Center
Description: Facilities and equipment where rail transit communications are handled.

Name: Rail Transit Signaling Facility or Equipment
Description: Facilities and equipment to signal trains and direct traffic of trains in transit.

Name: Bus Mass Transit
Description: Mass transit operating fixed routes and schedules on streets shared with other vehicles and pedestrians.

Name: Transit Bus Vehicle
Description: Bus vehicles powered by diesel, gasoline, battery or alternative fuel engines contained within the vehicle. Can be single unit or articulated. Trolleybus vehicles propelled by a motor drawing current from overhead wires via a connecting pole called a trolley from a central power source not on board the vehicle.

Name: Transit Bus Route
Description: Routes followed by transit buses, usually on streets shared with other vehicles and pedestrians.

Name: Transit Bus Terminal
Description: Central facilities or hubs for buses to load and unload passengers. May have connections to other transport modes. Also called bus stations or bus depots.

Name: Transit Bus Stop
Description: Stops for loading and unloading passengers. May have a shelter.

Name: Transit Bus Garage
Description: Storage and maintenance facilities for transit buses.

Name: Transit Bus Dispatch or Operations Control Center
Description: Facilities where transit bus personnel monitor and control the movement of buses.

Name: Transit Bus Communication Center
Description: Facilities and equipment where bus communications are handled.

Name: Other Mass Transit Systems
Description: Mass transit facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Pipeline
Description: Pipelines for transporting liquids and gases. Includes petroleum and natural gas pipelines (both of which are also itemized in the Energy Sector), water pipelines (also itemized in the Water Sector), hazardous chemicals (also itemized in the Chemical and Hazardous Materials Sector), and other liquids and gases.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline
Description: Pipeline facilities for the transport of crude oil.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline Component
Description: Lengths of pipeline, interconnections, and valves. Includes above ground, under ground, river crossings, and other segments.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline Pumping Station
Description: Stations along the length of a pipeline. Includes pumps, valves, control machinery, and breakout storage.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate a pipeline(s). Includes supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system control centers.

Name: Crude Oil Storage
Description: Facilities used for the storage and/or marketing of crude oil. Includes storage tanks, pipes and pumps, control machinery, and other equipment. Does not include storage at refineries. Also referred to as tank farms.

Name: Crude Oil Pipeline Hub
Description: A market or supply area for pooling and delivery of crude oil where transactions occur to facilitate the movement of crude oil between and among interstate pipelines. Transactions can include a change in title of crude ownership, a change in crude transporter, or other similar actions. Also known as a Market Center.

Name: Petroleum Product Pipeline
Description: Pipeline facilities for the transport of petroleum products.

Name: Petroleum Product Pipeline Component or Interconnect
Description: Lengths of pipeline, interconnections, and valves. Includes above ground, under ground, river crossings, and other segments. Facilities that link one company to another company to transfer products or custody or provide emergency transportation service between companies. This includes facilities such as pipeline segments, valves, or pressure reduction stations.

Name: Petroleum Product Pipeline Pumping Station
Description: Stations along the length of a pipeline. Includes pumps, valves, control machinery, and breakout storage.

Name: Petroleum Product Pipeline Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate a pipeline(s). Includes supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system control centers.

Name: Petroleum Product Storage
Description: Facilities used for the storage and/or marketing of petroleum products. Includes storage tanks, pipes and pumps, control machinery, and other equipment. Does not include storage at refineries. Also referred to as tank farms.

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline
Description: Large, high-volume pipelines.

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Component or Interconnect
Description: Lengths of pipeline, interconnections, and valves. Includes above ground, under ground, river crossings, and other segments. This includes facilities that link one company to another company to transfer gas custody or provide emergency transportation service between companies. Also included are facilities such as pipeline segments, valves, or metering and or pressure reduction stations.
CrosssectorReference:
5.3.5.1.1 Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Segment
5.3.5.1.3 Natural Gas Pipeline Interconnect

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Compressor Station
Description: Stations along the length of a transmission pipeline. Includes gas-powered or electric compressors, valves, control systems, and associated equipment.
CrosssectorReference: 5.3.5.1.2 Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Compressor Station

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate a transmission pipeline(s). Generally includes supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) equipment.
CrosssectorReference: 5.3.5.1.6 Natural Gas Transmission Pipeline Control Center

Name: Natural Gas Transmission Storage
Description: Facilities for storing natural gas.

Name: Natural Gas Pipeline Hub
Description: A market or supply area for pooling and delivery of gas where transactions occur to facilitate the movement of gas between and among interstate pipelines. Transactions can include a change in title of gas ownership, a change in gas transporter, aggregation of gas supply, or other similar items. Also known as a Market Center.

Name: Natural Gas Receipt/Delivery Metering Station
Description: Gas custody transfer metering stations along transmission pipelines. Used to monitor the amount of gas that is transported and to provide quantity measurements for billing purposes.

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas Storage Terminal
Description: Facilities that store liquefied natural gas (LNG) and regasify it for injection into pipelines. Includes specially designed tanks to store the LNG.
CrosssectorReference: 5.3.3.2 Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Satellite Storage and  Regasification Plant

Name: Natural Gas Distribution
Description: Facilities, generally owned by a local distribution company (LDC), that distribute natural gas to final consumers.

Name: City Gate Station
Description: Facilities where a local distribution company (LDC) receives gas from a transmission company. Activities at the facility include: measuring, custody transfer, pressure regulating, and odorization to meet distribution network requirements.

Name: Natural Gas Distribution Pipeline Network
Description: The network of lower pressure pipelines that provide natural gas to consumers.

Name: Natural Gas Distribution Control or Dispatch Center
Description: Centers that control the lower pressure gas distribution system. Includes distribution supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems.
CrosssectorReference: 5.3.6.3 Natural Gas Distribution Control and Dispatch Center

Name: Natural Gas Distribution Storage
Description: Natural gas distribution storage facilities for storing natural gas for peak shaving and distribution.

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Transport
Description: Transport facilities to move liquefied natural gas (LNG).

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Tanker
Description: Specially-designed ships for carrying liquefied natural gas (LNG) and maintaining very low temperatures. Generally used for imported LNG.

Name: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Port
Description: Port facilities designed to handle liquefied natural gas (LNG) tankers. Includes mooring facilities, loading and unloading facilities, specially designed storage tanks, and regasification equipment to regasify LNG for injection into pipelines.

Name: Other Pipelines
Description: Pipeline facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Other Pipeline Component
Description: Lengths of pipeline, interconnections, and valves. Includes above ground, under ground, river crossings, and other segments.

Name: Other Pipeline Pumping Station
Description: Stations along the length of a pipeline. Includes pumps, valves, control machinery, and breakout storage.

Name: Other Pipeline Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate a pipeline(s). Includes supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system control centers.

Name: Other Pipeline Terminal
Description: Facilities where multiple pipelines interconnect. May include storage facilities where material being transported is stored temporarily.

Name: Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Organizations that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight support to the Transportation Sector.

Name: Federal Transportation Agency
Description: Federal agencies that deal with transportation such as Department of Transportation, Federal Aviation Administration, U.S. Coast Guard, U.S. Army Corps of Engineers.

Name: State, Local, or Regional Transportation Agency
Description: State, local, and regional agencies that deal with transportation in their jurisdictions.

Name: Transportation Industry Organization
Description: Industry organizations that provide industry-wide support.

Name: International Transportation Organization
Description: International organizations dealing with transportation issues

Name: Water
Description: The Water Sector includes both drinking water and wastewater utilities and systems.

Name: Raw Water Supply
Description: Sources for raw water that are used by commercial and private water purveyors to supply drinking water.  Note: Some water supplies may be ground water, surface water, or a combination of the two. The vast majority of community water systems serving fewer than 10,000 people use ground water as their source. Large community water systems obtain most of their water from surface sources.

Name: Surface Water
Description: Sources of raw water that are located above ground that are used to supply water, including lakes, reservoirs, and rivers.

Name: Ground Water
Description: Sources of raw water that are located below ground (e.g., aquifers) and are used to supply water. This may include drainage basins or watersheds.

Name: Raw Water Transmission
Description: Facilities that are used to convey water from a remote raw water source to a treatment plant. Community water systems may use pipes or open canals (aqueducts). The water may be pumped or gravity-fed.

Name: Aqueduct
Description: Open-channel conduits used to convey water from a source area to a treatment facility.

Name: Water Pipeline
Description: Closed conduits (e.g., pipes, tunnels) used to convey water from a source area to a treatment facility.

Name: Water Pipeline Segment
Description: Lengths of pipeline, interconnections, and valves. Includes above ground, under ground, river crossings, and other segments.

Name: Water Pipeline Pumping Station
Description: Stations along the length of a pipeline. Includes pumps, valves, control machinery, and breakout storage.

Name: Catch Basin
Description: In a combined sewer system, surface-level inlets where excess wastewater and run-off is frequently discharged to a holding basin for subsequent treatment and disposal.

Name: Raw Water Storage
Description: Facilities that store raw water from a source before it is treated.

Name: Raw Water Storage Reservoir
Description: Reservoirs used to hold raw water prior to treatment. May be in remote or urban areas.

Name: Raw Water Storage Tank
Description: Tanks used to hold raw water prior to treatment.

Name: Water Treatment Facility
Description: Facilities used to provide safe, potable water for domestic use, adequate quantities of water at sufficient pressure for fire protection and other emergencies, and industrial water for manufacturing. A variety of physical and chemical treatments are applied. Steps in treatment may include clarification, coagulation, sedimentation, filtration, disinfection, and fluoridation.

Name: Treated (Finished) Water Storage
Description: Facilities to store treated (e.g., finished) water for a short time prior to distribution to customers.

Name: Water Tower
Description: Elevated storage tanks that are used to provide additional system pressure by using gravity.

Name: Standpipe
Description: Flat-bottomed cylindrical tanks, with a shell height that is greater than its diameter, used for storing treated water.

Name: Covered Reservoir
Description: Flat-bottomed cylindrical tanks, with a shell height that is equal to or smaller than its diameter, used for storing treated water either above or below the ground surface.

Name: Uncovered Reservoir
Description: Reservoirs without covers used to store treated water.

Name: Clearwell
Description: Used to provide interim contact time during the water treatment process.

Name: Treated Water Distribution System
Description: Facilities that transport treated water to end users. This includes the network of pipes, tanks, pumps, and valves that convey and that adjust the flow such that the proper volume and pressure of water is delivered when and where needed.

Name: Water Transmission/Distribution Main
Description: Pipes used to transport large quantities of water within the distribution network.

Name: Water Service Line
Description: Pipes that transport smaller quantities of water in the distribution network to the end users.

Name: Water Distribution Pumping Station
Description: Facilities used to increase the pressure in the distribution network when end users are located far from the treatment plant.

Name: Fire Hydrant
Description: Devices used to provide access to the distribution network for obtaining emergency water, washing down streets, and flushing water mains.

Name: Booster Disinfection Facility
Description: Facilities that include pumps and valves used to inject additional disinfectant in the treated water to maintain desired water quality.

Name: Backflow Preventer
Description: Devices used to prevent contaminated water from entering the distribution network if the downstream pressure exceeds the pressure upstream.

Name: Meter
Description: Devices used to track consumption of water in the distribution network.

Name: Treated Water Monitoring System
Description: Facilities to monitor treated water quality for conventional regulated and unregulated contaminants. Some systems have sensors installed at critical points to monitor a range of physical properties, such as water pressure and water-quality indicators.

Name: Treated Water Distribution Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate the distribution system. Some systems have electronic networks, often including wireless communication, to link the monitoring system, and the controls for the treatment and distribution systems, to a central display and operations room. These systems may also help to automate control of a drinking water system with monitoring-system readouts serving as inputs for control. Supervisory control and data acquisition systems are part of an integrated control system.

Name: Wastewater Facility
Description: Facilities used to collect and treat wastewater for safe discharge or reuse, and disposal of produced biosolids.

Name: Wastewater Collection System
Description: The network of pipes that conveys wastewater from the source to the treatment plant. In some older cities, the wastewater and storm water collection systems are integrated (combined sewer systems), and in wet weather the combined effluent may discharge directly to the receiving body, bypassing the treatment plant.

Name: Sewer
Description: Underground conduits used to collect and convey wastewater by gravity flow from an urban area to points of disposal or treatment. Laterals connect residences and industries to branch sewers, sub-main lines, and main line sewers (also called trunks or force mains, if driven by a pump). This may include outfalls and catch basins.

Name: Inverted Siphon
Description: A sewer that is buried deeper below ground in order to avoid an obstruction such as a stream, railway cut, or depressed highway.

Name: Manhole
Description: Openings to the sewer system that are used for inspection and cleaning.

Name: Combined Sewer Overflow Outfall
Description: For combined sewer systems, the location where excess wastewater and run-off is frequently discharged directly to a receiving stream without treatment.

Name: Lift/Pump Station
Description: Stations are used to elevate and transport wastewater in collection systems using a system of pumps when gravity flow is insufficient or no longer feasible.

Name: Catch Basin
Description: In a combined sewer system, surface-level inlets where excess wastewater and run-off is frequently discharged to a holding basin for subsequent treatment and disposal.

Name: Wastewater Raw Influent Storage
Description: Facilities to store raw sewage and/or industrial effluents prior to treatment. Generally used for the purpose of flow equalization. May be tanks or impoundments.

Name: Wastewater Treatment Plant
Description: Plants providing a combination of physical and biological processes that are designed to remove organic matter from solution and treat the water to a degree that it can be released to the environment. Processes include screening, grit removal, flotation, flocculation and sedimentation, aeration, clarification, disinfection, chemical coagulation, and filtration. The processes are applied to the plant influent to reduce pollutant levels to the concentrations specified in the National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) permit, in the case of a direct discharger, or other specified discharge limits, in the case of an indirect discharger.

Name: Treated Wastewater Storage
Description: Facilities where treated wastewater is held (e.g., in lagoons, detention basins) prior to discharge.

Name: Treated Wastewater Discharge System
Description: Facilities that discharge treated wastewater to a surface water body, in the case of a direct discharger, or to a publicly owned treatment works (POTW) collection system, in the case of an indirect discharger.

Name: Treated Wastewater Monitoring System
Description: Facilities to monitor a range of physical properties, such as flow rates and water-quality indicators, and detect levels of contaminants before, during, and after wastewater treatment.

Name: Wastewater Control Center
Description: Central control facilities that monitor and operate the wastewater system. Some systems have electronic networks, often including wireless communication, to link the monitoring system, and the controls for the treatment and distribution systems, to a central display and operations room. These systems may also help to automate control of a drinking water system with monitoring-system readouts serving as inputs for control. Supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems are part of an integrated control system.

Name: Regulatory, Oversight, or Industry Organization
Description: Organizations that provide technical, operation, pricing, and business oversight and support to the various components of the water system.

Name: Federal Water Agency
Description: Federal agencies dealing with water including Environmental Protection Agency, U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, etc.

Name: State, Local, or Regional Water Agency
Description: State, local, and regional agencies that deal with water in their jurisdictions.

Name: Water Industry Organization
Description: Industry organizations that provide industry-wide support.

Name: International Water Organization
Description: International organizations dealing with water issues.

Name: Private-Sector Water Supply or Distribution Company
Description: Commercial water companies contracted by a district to provide water and/or wastewater services. These companies may either own the facilities or operate municipal facilities.

Name: National Monuments and Icons
Description: Assets including National Monuments, symbols, and icons that represent the nation’s heritage, traditions, values, and political power. This sector includes a wide variety of sites and structures such as prominent historical attractions, monuments, cultural icons, and centers of commerce and business. Includes assets with significance at the local, regional, or national level as well as those that are government (federal, state, local) and privately owned. These assets Include structures, geographical areas, documents, and objects. Many assets are listed in the Department of Interior’s national register of historic places or national historic landmarks. Note: Some assets are only categorized in this category (e.g., Statue of Liberty, Washington Monument). Other assets are primarily identified as part of another sector but also have icon status (e.g., Golden Gate Bridge, U.S. Capitol). These assets have both their primary categorization and this icon categorization.

Name: National Monument/Icon Structure
Description: National monument/icon assets that are structures.  May or may not be located in a monument/icon geographic area.

Name: Building
Description: Assets that are buildings.  Includes historic buildings, homes, and others.

Name: Monument or Memorial
Description: Assets that are monuments, memorials, statues, and others.

Name: Other National Monument/Icon Structure
Description: Assets other than buildings and monuments (e.g., famous bridges).

Name: National Monument/Icon Geographic Area
Description: National monument/icon assets that are primarily geographical areas.  May or may not contain any structures.

Name: Park or Public Land
Description: National, state and local parks, forests, and recreational areas.

Name: Historical or Heritage Land
Description: Historic battlefields, landmarks, and others.

Name: Other National Monument/Icon Geographic Area
Description: Assets other than parks and historical areas (e.g., commercial districts).

Name: National Monument/Icon Document or Object
Description: National monument/icon assets including historical documents and historical objects.  Generally on display in museums, libraries, historic sites, and other such locations.  They would retain their iconic value if temporarily moved to another location, e.g., for an exhibition.

Name: Historical/Significant Document
Description: Documents of historical significance nationally, regionally, or locally (e.g., Declaration of Independence, United States Constitution).

Name: Historical/Significant Object
Description: Objects of historical or other significance nationally, regionally, or locally (e.g., Liberty Bell).

Name: Other National Monuments and Icons
Description: National monument and icon facilities and assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Commercial Facilities
Description: Includes commercial businesses and community facilities. Most are privately owned, but some are publicly owned and operated.

Name: Entertainment or Media Facility
Description: Facilities engaged in producing and distributing information and cultural products, processing data, and providing the means to transmit or distribute these products, data or communications.

Name: Television Production Facility
Description: Establishments involved in the production and distribution of television programming. This may or may not include broadcasting.

Name: Internet Publishing
Description: Establishments engaged in publishing and/or broadcasting content on the Internet exclusively. Establishments in this industry provide textual, audio, and/or video content of general or specific interest on the Internet. Includes Internet host web sites. Does not include the hardware and software to operate the Internet system, which are categorized under the Information Technology and Telecommunications sectors.

Name: Motion Picture or Sound Recording Facility
Description: Establishments involved in the production and distribution of motion pictures and sound recordings.

Name: Print Media
Description: Facilities engaged in publication of printed materials.

Name: Newspaper and Periodical Publishing
Description: Facilities engaged in preparing, printing, and distributing newspapers, magazines, and other periodicals.

Name: Other Print Publishing
Description: Facilities engaged in preparing, printing, and distributing non-periodical material including books, directories, and other material.

Name: Gaming Facility/Casino
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in operating gaming facilities that offer table wagering games along with other gaming activities, such as slot machines and sports betting. Often provide food and beverage services.  May be attached to a hotel.

Name: Gaming Complex
Description: Establishments that are either land- or water-based primarily engaged in gaming activities that are part of a complex that includes lodging, convention facilities, entertainment, and shopping facilities.

Name: Stand Alone Casino
Description: Casinos that have no other functions such as lodging, shopping, or convention facilities.

Name: Permanently-Moored Casino
Description: Casinos operating from a permanently moored vessel and/or have been taken out of navigation.

Name: Other Gaming Establishments
Description: Includes bingo parlors, slot machine parlors, video gaming device operators, and lottery ticket vendors. May be located in other facilities such as hotels.

Name: Lodging Facility
Description: Facilities providing lodging or short-term accommodations for travelers, vacationers, and others.

Name: Bed and Breakfast Inn
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in providing short-term lodging in private homes or small buildings converted for this purpose.

Name: Hotel or Motel
Description: Includes hotels, motels, motor hotels, lodges, spas, resorts, and seasonal lodges.  May have other facilities associated (e.g., restaurants, golf courses, casinos, conference, convention facilities)

Name: Other Lodging Facility
Description: Other types of lodging including cabins, cottages, hostels, etc.

Name: Public Assembly
Description: Facilities where large numbers of people congregate to pursue business activities, conduct personal transactions, or enjoy recreational pastimes.

Name: Motor Racetrack
Description: Facilities that include specially surfaced circuits or courses used for motor racing. The track is a permanent facility and may feature grandstands and concourses.

Name: Horse or Dog Track
Description: Facilities that include specially surfaced courses built for the racing of horses or dogs. The track is a permanent facility and may feature grandstands or concourses.

Name: Arena
Description: Large indoor facilities in which events are held for large audiences. Generally designed primarily for spectator sports but also with the ability to host concerts, lectures, rallies, circuses and other events with large audiences.

Name: Stadium
Description: Large outdoor facilities in which events are held for large audiences.  May or may not have retractable domes for dealing with inclement weather. Generally designed primarily for spectator sports but also with the ability to host concerts, lectures, rallies, circuses and other events with large audiences.

Name: Amphitheater
Description: Venues for concerts or theatrical performances with rising tiers of seats arranged about an open space.  Amphitheaters can be man-made or a geological formation that has been converted to a facility.

Name: Museum or Planetarium
Description: Facilities engaged in the preservation and exhibition of objects, sites, and natural wonders of historical, cultural, and/or educational value.

Name: Performing Arts Center or Auditorium
Description: Indoor facilities in which performing arts are presented to audiences. Includes concert halls, live theatrical production theaters, dance theaters, community theaters, and other such facilities. Generally smaller than arenas.

Name: Zoo, Aquarium, or Botanical Garden
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in the preservation and exhibition of live plant and animal life displays.

Name: Convention Center
Description: Facilities where large groups of people (e.g., political party, professional organization, civic group) meet to exchange information and where trade shows displaying products are held.  May also host other events such as concerts, theater performances, and sporting events.  May be attached to other facilities such as hotels.

Name: Golf Course or Country Club
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in operating golf courses. May include dining facilities and other recreational facilities that are known as country clubs.

Name: Movie Theater
Description: Facilities in which motion pictures are shown.  May have multiple screens.

Name: Entertainment District
Description: Areas with a concentration of different types of entertainment facilities in close proximity.  May include restaurants, nightclubs, and other such establishments.

Name: Sports Recreational Facility
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in operating fitness and recreational sports facilities, such as natatoriums, multi-sports complexes, and racquet courts.

Name: Community Organization Facility
Description: Facilities where members of a community gather for social, educational, religious, political, recreational, or other purposes.  May be publicly or privately operated facility.

Name: Amusement, Theme, or Water Park
Description: Entertainment facilities featuring rides, games, food, and sometimes shows.  Includes theme parks in which the attractions revolve around a central theme or group of themes, traditional amusement parks in which the activities do not focus on a common theme, and water parks in which the attractions center on water-based rides and activities.

Name: Community Park, Fairground, Pavilion
Description: Community areas open to the public.  Does not include parklands identified within Sector 13, National Monuments and Icons.

Name: Community Water Facility
Description: Beaches, piers, boardwalks, and swimming pools open to the public.

Name: Community Special Gathering Venues
Description: Areas, facilities, or outdoor venues where parades, festivals, carnivals, scenic tours, holiday events, and demonstration protests can take place.

Name: Parade Route
Description: Parades scheduled in advance along a pre-determined route.

Name: Special Event Venue
Description: Facilities or outdoor venues where festivals, carnivals, scenic tours, or holiday events can take place. Generally scheduled well in advance.

Name: Marina
Description: Marinas are primarily covered in Sector 11, Transportation. This section is concerned with the land based operations associated with a marina such as the docking and/or storage facilities for pleasure craft owners, retailing marine supplies, repairing, maintaining, or renting pleasure boats, and shopping areas or districts associated or adjacent to the marina. Includes boating and sailing clubs, and yacht basins. Other marina activities such as the docks, regulation of water activities, and commercial shipping, navigation and fishing are covered in the Transportation Sector.

Name: Mountain Facility
Description: Establishments engaged in operating downhill or cross-country skiing, or other outdoor recreation facilities.

Name: Recreational Vehicle Park or Recreational Camp
Description: Establishments primarily engaged in operating recreational vehicle parks, campgrounds, or recreational vacation camps. These are characterized by the type of accommodation and by the nature and range of recreational facilities and activities provided.

Name: Other Public Assembly Facility
Description: Other public assembly facilities not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Real Estate Facility
Description: Facilities that are owned and/or operated by commercial stakeholders.  May be leased to business or residential tenants.

Name: Office Building
Description: Facilities housing business offices.  Includes small single-story offices for small businesses and large multi-story office buildings housing a number of tenants.

Name: Office Building - Stand Alone
Description: Individual office buildings.  May be single- or multi-tenant.

Name: Office District
Description: A number of office buildings that are in a common area ( e.g., central business district) but are not under common management.  May not have dedicated parking.

Name: Office Park
Description: Groupings of office buildings in a common area.  Buildings generally under common management for operation and maintenance.  May have dedicated parking.

Name: Residential Unit
Description: Residential facilities including single and multi-family.

Name: Multi-Family Residence
Description: Residences with more than one household.

Name: Single-Family Residence
Description: Residences with a notable owner or that are a significant structure.

Name: Self-Storage Facility
Description: Establishments engaged in renting space, used for storing personal and household items, typically known as units, rooms, or lockers.

Name: Mixed Use Property
Description: Facilities that combine more than one type of use, e.g., residential and commercial, in a building or set of buildings.

Name: Retail Facility
Description: This comprises two main types of retailers: store and non-store retailers.

Name: Store Retailer
Description: Establishments operating fixed point-of-sale locations, located and designed to attract a high volume of walk-in customers.

Name: Shopping Center, Mall, or Shopping District
Description: A number of stores in a central facility or in a common area ( e.g., a downtown shopping district, commercial shopping area along a roadway).  May or may not have dedicated parking.  Includes shopping centers that are located on large plots of land and smaller "strip malls" that are located along roads.  May include recreational and entertainment facilities ( e.g., theaters, game arcades) and/or restaurants.

Name: Stand-Alone Store
Description: Stores that are separated from other stores.  May be large facilities with dedicated parking or small independent shops in an isolated location.

Name: Non-Store Retailer
Description: Establishments serving the general public without using fixed store locations.  Includes merchandise retailed by catalogs, television "infomercials", Internet advertising, portable stalls, and other such techniques.  Generally includes a warehouse location where merchandise is stored and shipped from.

Name: Other Commercial Facility
Description: Commercial assets not elsewhere categorized.

Name: Government Facilities
Description: Buildings and structures associated and owned, leased, or otherwise acquired by federal, state, local, tribal, and territorial government agencies.

Name: Personnel-Oriented Government Facility
Description: Government facilities with the primary function of housing personnel in the course of their official business or personal pursuits.

Name: Personnel-Oriented Building or Structure
Description: Government buildings primarily used to accommodate personnel.

Name: Office or Office Building Complex
Description: Government buildings used for office space.

Name: Agency Headquarters
Description: Buildings housing the headquarters and chief executives of government departments or agencies.

Name: Field, District, or Satellite Office
Description: Field offices that house federal, state, or local agency operations that are agency specific or administrative in nature.

Name: Legislative Chamber or Office
Description: Government buildings used to conduct and support the business of the legislative branch.

Name: Judicial Chamber or Office
Description: Government buildings used to conduct and support the business of the judicial branch, including courthouses.

Name: Data or Call Center
Description: Government buildings providing data warehousing or informational support to department, agency, legislative, or judicial functions.

Name: Housing
Description: Government buildings primarily used for living space.  Includes government-owned detached and attached houses, apartment buildings, and barracks whose primary purpose is to provide living quarters for government employees.

Name: Correctional Facility
Description: Government buildings designed for the confinement, correction, and rehabilitation of adult and/or juvenile offenders sentenced by a court. Includes correctional institutions, detention centers, community corrections centers, and holding cells in courthouses.

Name: Embassy or Consulate
Description: Government buildings used to facilitate foreign relations. Includes United States government embassies and consulates located in foreign countries, and foreign government embassies and consulates located in the United States.

Name: Border Facility
Description: Government buildings used at border crossing sites that provide immigration and customs control at the borders shared with Canada and Mexico.

Name: Immigration and Customs (Airport, Seaport)
Description: Airports and seaports used for immigration and customs purposes.

Name: Personnel-Oriented Land
Description: Government land surrounding and supporting the function and activities of personnel-centric buildings or planned buildings and structures.  Includes areas used to provide training on weapons systems.  Also includes land held in trust by the government.

Name: Service-Oriented Government Facility
Description: Facilities geared towards providing services to government and the public.  These facilities typically experience large numbers of visitors and house technical equipment necessary to carry out and deliver government services.

Name: Service-Oriented Building or Structure
Description: Buildings geared towards providing services to government and the public.

Name: Emergency Services
Description: Government buildings used to provide emergency services.

Name: Maintenance or Repair Shop
Description: Government buildings used for the upkeep and repair of government property. Includes vehicle maintenance and service stations.

Name: Operations, Command, Dispatch, or Control Center
Description: Government buildings used to control and coordinate personnel and operations over a specific area.

Name: Training Building
Description: Government buildings used to conduct specialized training for personnel to conduct government functions.

Name: Library
Description: Government buildings used to store and facilitate public access to reference and reading material. This includes public and presidential libraries.

Name: Service-Oriented Land
Description: Land associated with service-oriented buildings and structures. Includes land used for parking areas attached to highways without fuel or maintenance facilities.

Name: Government Research Facility
Description: Specialized facilities to conduct research.  Includes government facilities and government-owned-contractor-operated (GOCO) facilities.  Categorized by the type of research conducted.

Name: Government Research and Development Building or Structure
Description: Specialized buildings to conduct research and development activities.

Name: Analysis and Assessment Research Facility
Description: Government buildings where studies and computer analyses are primarily conducted.  These buildings may have significant computer facilities and do not include laboratory or experimental facilities.

Name: Environmental Research
